Arabic: سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ
Translation: Glory be to Allah
Benefit Text: Whoever says SubhanAllah 100 times, his sins are wiped even if like the foam of the sea.
Illustration: Wooden tasbih beads glowing one after another as a hand counts, soft light rising with each bead.

Arabic: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ
Translation: All praise is for Allah
Benefit Text: Alhamdulillah fills the scales of good deeds.
Illustration: A golden scale tipping fully to the side of good deeds, soft warm light radiating from the heavier pan.

Arabic: اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ
Translation: Allah is the Greatest
Benefit Text: SubhanAllah, Alhamdulillah, Allahu Akbar — more beloved than all that is under the sun.
Illustration: Wooden tasbih beads glowing one after another as a hand counts, soft light rising with each bead.

Arabic: لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ
Translation: There is no god but Allah
Benefit Text: Whoever says it 100 times: reward of freeing 10 slaves, 100 good deeds written, 100 sins erased, protected from Shaytan.
Illustration: Ten chains breaking and falling from a freed wrist, a white dove rising into warm dawn light.

Arabic: لَا حَوْلَ وَلَا قُوَّةَ إِلَّا بِاللَّهِ
Translation: There is no power nor strength except with Allah
Benefit Text: It is a treasure from the treasures of Paradise.
Illustration: A sealed golden treasure-chest of Paradise just beginning to open, a single key glowing with the words inscribed upon it.

Arabic: سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ وَبِحَمْدِهِ
Translation: Glory be to Allah and His is the praise
Benefit Text: Whoever says it 100 times morning and evening — comes on Judgment Day with nothing better except one who said more.
Illustration: A vast ocean horizon at dawn, foam softly dissolving into the rising sunlight on the surface.

Arabic: سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ الْعَظِيمِ
Translation: Glory be to Allah, the Almighty
Benefit Text: Two phrases light on the tongue, heavy on the scales, beloved to the Most Merciful.
Illustration: A golden scale tipped fully to one side beneath a swirling galaxy of countless tasbih beads.

Arabic: بِسْمِ اللّٰهِ الرَّحْمٰنِ الرَّحِيْمِ اَلْحَمْدُ لِلّٰهِ رَبِّ الْعَالَمِيْنَ ، الرَّحْمٰنِ الرَّحِيْمِ ، مَالِكِ يَوْمِ الدِّيْنِ ، إِيَّاكَ نَعْبُدُ وَإِيَّاكَ نَسْتَعِيْنُ ، اِهْدِنَا الصِّرَاطَ الْمُسْتَقِيْمَ ، صِرَاطَ الَّذِيْنَ أَنْعَمْتَ عَلَيْهِمْ غَيْرِ الْمَغْضُوْبِ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلَا الضَّالِّيْنَ
Translation: In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful. All praise is due to Allah, Lord of the worlds. The Most Gracious, the Most Merciful. Master of the Day of Judgment. You alone we worship, and You alone we ask for help. Guide us to the straight path — the path of those upon whom You have bestowed favor, not of those who have evoked Your anger or of those who are astray.
Benefit Text: Al Fateha
Illustration: Open Quran turned to Surah Al-Fatihah, gentle light radiating outward from the calligraphy onto open palms.

Arabic: الٓمٓ ، ذٰلِكَ الْكِتَابُ لَا رَيْبَ فِيْهِ هُدًى لِّلْمُتَّقِيْنَ ، الَّذِيْنَ يُؤْمِنُوْنَ بِالْغَيْبِ وَيُقِيْمُوْنَ الصَّلَاةَ وَمِمَّا رَزَقْنَاهُمْ يُنْفِقُوْنَ ، وَالَّذِيْنَ يُؤْمِنُوْنَ بِمَا أُنْزِلَ إِلَيْكَ وَمَا أُنْزِلَ مِنْ قَبْلِكَ وَبِالْآخِرَةِ هُمْ يُوْقِنُوْنَ ، أُولٰئِكَ عَلَىٰ هُدًى مِنْ رَبِّهِمْ وَأُولٰئِكَ هُمُ الْمُفْلِحُوْنَ
Translation: Alif, Lam, Mim. This is the Book about which there is no doubt, a guidance for those conscious of Allah — who believe in the unseen, establish prayer, and spend out of what We have provided for them, and who believe in what has been revealed to you and what was revealed before you, and of the Hereafter they are certain. Those are upon guidance from their Lord, and it is those who are the successful.
Benefit Text: Alif Laam Meem Ad-Darimi
Illustration: Open Quran with the opening of Surah Al-Baqarah glowing, soft path of guidance light leading into the page.

Arabic: أَعُوْذُ بِاللّٰهِ مِنَ الشَّيْطَانِ الرَّجِيْمِ. اَللّٰهُ لَآ إِلٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ الْحَىُّ الْقَيُّوْمُ ، لَا تَأْخُذُهُۥ سِنَةٌ وَّلَا نَوْمٌ ، لَهُ مَا فِى السَّمٰـوٰتِ وَمَا فِى الْأَرْضِ ، مَنْ ذَا الَّذِىْ يَشْفَعُ عِنْدَهُ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِهِۦ ، يَعْلَمُ مَا بَيْنَ أَيْدِيْهِمْ وَمَا خَلْفَهُمْ ، وَلَا يُحِيْطُوْنَ بِشَىْءٍ مِّنْ عِلْمِهِٓ إِلَّا بِمَا شَآءَ ، وَسِعَ كُرْسِيُّهُ السَّمٰـوٰتِ وَالْأَرْضَ، وَلَا يَئُوْدُهُۥ حِفْظُهُمَا ، وَهُوَ الْعَلِىُّ الْعَظِيْمُ.
Translation: I seek the protection of Allah from the accursed Shayṭān. Allah, there is no god worthy of worship but He, the Ever Living, The Sustainer of all. Neither drowsiness overtakes Him nor sleep. To Him Alone belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. Who is it that can intercede with Him except with His permission? He knows what is before them and what will be after them, and they encompass not a thing of His knowledge except for what He wills. His Kursī extends over the heavens and the earth, and their preservation does not tire Him. And He is the Most High, the Magnificent. (2:255)
Benefit Text: Ayatul Kursi
Illustration: Glowing celestial throne extending across the heavens and earth, with a soft golden dome of protection forming over a kneeling worshipper.

Arabic: لَا إِكْرَاهَ فِي الدِّيْنِ ، قَدْ تَبَيَّنَ الرُّشْدُ مِنَ الْغَيِّ ، فَمَنْ يَّكْفُرْ بِالطَّاغُوْتِ وَيُؤْمِنْ بِاللّٰهِ فَقَدِ اسْتَمْسَكَ بِالْعُرْوَةِ الْوُثْقَىٰ لَا انْفِصَامَ لَهَا ، وَاللّٰهُ سَمِيْعٌ عَلِيْمٌ
Translation: There shall be no compulsion in the religion. The right course has become clear from the wrong. So whoever disbelieves in false deities and believes in Allah has grasped the most trustworthy handhold with no break in it. And Allah is Hearing and Knowing.
Benefit Text: There are no specific conditions or requirements to recite this Quranic ayah as part of the morning or evening adhkar. However, this ayah is included in Ruqya and can be added as a supplementary recitation.
Illustration: Two open palms gently raised above a softly glowing chest, soft blue healing light flowing from the hands into the heart.

Arabic: اَللّٰهُ وَلِيُّ الَّذِيْنَ آمَنُوْا يُخْرِجُهُمْ مِّنَ الظُّلُمَاتِ إِلَى النُّوْرِ ، وَالَّذِيْنَ كَفَرُوْا أَوْلِيَاؤُهُمُ الطَّاغُوْتُ يُخْرِجُوْنَهُمْ مِّنَ النُّوْرِ إِلَى الظُّلُمَاتِ ، أُولٰئِكَ أَصْحَابُ النَّارِ هُمْ فِيْهَا خَالِدُوْنَ
Translation: Allah is the ally of those who believe. He brings them out from darknesses into the light. And those who disbelieve — their allies are false deities. They take them out of the light into darknesses. Those are the companions of the Fire; they will abide eternally therein.
Benefit Text: There are no specific conditions or requirements to recite this Quranic ayah as part of the morning or evening adhkar. However, this ayah is included in Ruqya and can be added as a supplementary recitation.
Illustration: Two open palms gently raised above a softly glowing chest, soft blue healing light flowing from the hands into the heart.

Arabic: لِلّٰهِ مَا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَمَا فِي الْأَرْضِ ، وَإِنْ تُبْدُوْا مَا فِيْ أَنْفُسِكُمْ أَوْ تُخْفُوْهُ يُحَاسِبْكُمْ بِهِ اللّٰهُ ، فَيَغْفِرُ لِمَنْ يَّشَاءُ وَيُعَذِّبُ مَنْ يَّشَاءُ ، وَاللّٰهُ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيْرٌ
Translation: To Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is in the earth. Whether you show what is within yourselves or conceal it, Allah will bring you to account for it. Then He will forgive whom He wills and punish whom He wills, and Allah is over all things competent.
Benefit Text: To Allah Belongs All
Illustration: A glowing key of dominion suspended in a starlit sky above an open landscape of mountains, oceans, and earth.

Arabic: آمَنَ الرَّسُوْلُ بِمَا أُنْزِلَ إِلَيْهِ مِنْ رَّبِّهِ وَالْمُؤْمِنُوْنَ ، كُلٌّ آمَنَ بِاللّٰهِ وَمَلَائِكَتِهِ وَكُتُبِهِ وَرُسُلِهِ لَا نُفَرِّقُ بَيْنَ أَحَدٍ مِّنْ رُسُلِهِ ، وَقَالُوْا سَمِعْنَا وَأَطَعْنَا غُفْرَانَكَ رَبَّنَا وَإِلَيْكَ الْمَصِيْرُ
Translation: The Messenger has believed in what was revealed to him from his Lord, and so have the believers. All of them have believed in Allah and His angels and His books and His messengers, saying, "We make no distinction between any of His messengers." And they say, "We hear and we obey. Grant us Your forgiveness, our Lord, and to You is the final destination."
Benefit Text: The Messenger Has Believed
Illustration: Cupped hands holding a soft, weighted light of guidance, an unburdened figure standing upright beside.

Arabic: لَا يُكَلِّفُ اللّٰهُ نَفْسًا إِلَّا وُسْعَهَا ، لَهَا مَا كَسَبَتْ وَعَلَيْهَا مَا اكْتَسَبَتْ ، رَبَّنَا لَا تُؤَاخِذْنَا إِنْ نَسِيْنَا أَوْ أَخْطَأْنَا ، رَبَّنَا وَلَا تَحْمِلْ عَلَيْنَا إِصْرًا كَمَا حَمَلْتَهُ عَلَى الَّذِيْنَ مِنْ قَبْلِنَا ، رَبَّنَا وَلَا تُحَمِّلْنَا مَا لَا طَاقَةَ لَنَا بِهِ ، وَاعْفُ عَنَّا وَاغْفِرْ لَنَا وَارْحَمْنَا ، أَنْتَ مَوْلَانَا فَانْصُرْنَا عَلَى الْقَوْمِ الْكَافِرِيْنَ
Translation: Allah does not burden a soul beyond that it can bear. It will have the consequence of what good it has gained, and it will bear the consequence of what evil it has earned. "Our Lord, do not impose blame upon us if we have forgotten or erred. Our Lord, and lay not upon us a burden like that which You laid upon those before us. Our Lord, and burden us not with that which we have no ability to bear. And pardon us; and forgive us; and have mercy upon us. You are our protector, so give us victory over the disbelieving people."
Benefit Text: Allah Does Not Burden a Soul
Illustration: Cupped hands holding a soft, weighted light of guidance, an unburdened figure standing upright beside.

Arabic: بِسْمِ اللّٰهِ الرَّحْمٰنِ الرَّحِيْمِ قُلْ هُوَ اللّٰهُ أَحَدٌ ، اَللّٰهُ الصَّمَدُ ، لَمْ يَلِدْ وَلَمْ يُوْلَدْ ، وَلَمْ يَكُنْ لَّهُ كُفُوًا أَحَدٌ
Translation: In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful. Say, "He is Allah, the One. Allah, the Eternal Refuge. He neither begets nor is born, nor is there to Him any equivalent."
Benefit Text: Surah Al-Ikhlas
Illustration: Open Quran turned to Surah Al-Ikhlas, calligraphy glowing, a single soft ray of light rising from the page.

Arabic: بِسْمِ اللّٰهِ الرَّحْمٰنِ الرَّحِيْمِ قُلْ أَعُوْذُ بِرَبِّ الْفَلَقِ ، مِنْ شَرِّ مَا خَلَقَ ، وَمِنْ شَرِّ غَاسِقٍ إِذَا وَقَبَ ، وَمِنْ شَرِّ النَّفَّاثَاتِ فِي الْعُقَدِ ، وَمِنْ شَرِّ حَاسِدٍ إِذَا حَسَدَ
Translation: In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful. Say, "I seek refuge in the Lord of daybreak, from the evil of that which He created, and from the evil of darkness when it settles, and from the evil of the blowers in knots, and from the evil of an envier when he envies."
Benefit Text: Surah Al-Falaq
Illustration: Open Quran on Surah Al-Falaq, dawn breaking on the horizon, a dark knot of evil dissolving into the morning light.

Arabic: بِسْمِ اللّٰهِ الرَّحْمٰنِ الرَّحِيْمِ قُلْ أَعُوْذُ بِرَبِّ النَّاسِ ، مَلِكِ النَّاسِ ، إِلٰهِ النَّاسِ ، مِنْ شَرِّ الْوَسْوَاسِ الْخَنَّاسِ ، الَّذِيْ يُوَسْوِسُ فِيْ صُدُوْرِ النَّاسِ ، مِنَ الْجِنَّةِ وَالنَّاسِ
Translation: In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful. Say, "I seek refuge in the Lord of mankind, the Sovereign of mankind, the God of mankind, from the evil of the retreating whisperer — who whispers in the breasts of mankind — from among the jinn and mankind."
Benefit Text: Surah An-Nas
Illustration: Open Quran on Surah An-Nas, a whispering shadow retreating from a softly glowing heart-silhouette.

Arabic: أَصْبَحْنَا وَأَصْبَحَ الْمُلْكُ لِلّٰهِ وَالْحَمْدُ لِلّٰهِ ، لَا إِلٰهَ إِلَّا اللّٰهُ وَحْدَهُ لَا شَرِيْكَ لَهُ ، لَهُ الْمُلْكُ وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ ، وَهُوَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيْرٌ ، رَبِّ أَسْأَلُكَ خَيْرَ مَا فِيْ هٰذَا الْيَوْمِ وَخَيْرَ مَا بَعْدَهُ ، وَأَعُوْذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا فِيْ هٰذَا الْيَوْمِ وَشَرِّ مَا بَعْدَهُ ، رَبِّ أَعُوْذُ بِكَ مِنَ الْكَسَلِ وَسُوْءِ الْكِبَرِ ، رَبِّ أَعُوْذُ بِكَ مِنْ عَذَابٍ فِي النَّارِ وَعَذَابٍ فِي الْقَبْرِ.
Translation: We have entered the morning and at this very time the whole kingdom belongs to Allah. All praise is due to Allah. There is no god worthy of worship except Allah, the One; He has no partner with Him. The entire kingdom belongs solely to Him, to Him is all praise due, and He is All-Powerful over everything. My Lord, I ask You for the good that is in this day and the good that follows it, and I seek Your protection from the evil that is in this day and from the evil that follows it. My Lord, I seek Your protection from laziness and the misery of old age. My Lord, I seek Your protection from the torment of the Hell-fire and the punishment of the grave.
Benefit Text: Dominion Belongs to Allah
Illustration: A simple white-shrouded form at peace under a soft starlit sky, a gentle column of mercy-light descending from above.

Arabic: أَصْبَحْنَا عَلَىٰ فِطْرَةِ الْإِسْلَامِ ، وَعَلَىٰ كَلِمَةِ الْإِخْلَاصِ ، وَعَلَىٰ دِيْنِ نَبِيِّنَا مُحَمَّدٍ ، وَعَلَىٰ مِلَّةِ أَبِيْنَا إِبْرَاهِيْمَ حَنِيْفًا مُّسْلِمًا وَّمَا كَانَ مِنَ الْمُشْرِكِيْنَ.
Translation: We have entered the morning upon the natural religion of Islam, the statement of pure faith (i.e. Shahādah), the religion of our Prophet Muhammad ﷺ and upon the way of our father Ibrāhīm, who turned away from all that is false, having surrendered to Allah, and he was not of the polytheists.
Benefit Text: Upon the Fitrah of Islam
Illustration: A small child-silhouette with a soft halo, surrounded by five gentle stars representing the pillars of Islam.

Arabic: اَللّٰهُمَّ بِكَ أَصْبَحْنَا وَبِكَ أَمْسَيْنَا وَبِكَ نَحْيَا وَبِكَ نَمُوْتُ وَإِلَيْكَ النُّشُوْرُ.
Translation: O Allah, by You we have entered the morning and by You we enter upon the evening. By You, we live and we die, and to You is the resurrection.
Benefit Text: By Your Leave
Illustration: Sunrise and sunset side by side on a continuous horizon, a soft path of light connecting the two.

Arabic: اَللّٰهُمَّ إِنِّيْ أَصْبَحْتُ مِنْكَ فِيْ نِعْمَةٍ وَعَافِيَةٍ وَسِتْرٍ ، فَأَتِمَّ عَلَيَّ نِعْمَتَكَ وَعَافِيَتَكَ وَسِتْرَكَ فِي الدُّنْيَا وَالْآخِرَةِ
Translation: O Allah, I have entered the morning receiving from You blessings, well-being and concealment (of my faults). So complete Your blessings upon me, Your well-being, and Your concealment in this world and the Hereafter.
Benefit Text: Blessings & Concealment Ibn al-Sunni
Illustration: Pink and white rose petals drifting upward toward elegant calligraphy of the Prophet’s name, soft gold halo around it.

Arabic: اَللّٰهُمَّ مَا أَصْبَحَ بِيْ مِنْ نِّعْمَةٍ أَوْ بِأَحَدٍ مِّنْ خَلْقِكَ ، فَمِنْكَ وَحْدَكَ لَا شَرِيْكَ لَكَ ، فَلَكَ الْحَمْدُ وَلَكَ الشُّكْرُ.
Translation: O Allah, all the favours that I or anyone from Your creation has received in the morning, are from You Alone. You have no partner. To You Alone belong all praise and all thanks.
Benefit Text: Fulfil Obligation of Gratitude
Illustration: Hands cupped to receive rays of light from above, tiny green seedlings sprouting from the palms.

Arabic: يَا رَبِّ لَكَ الْحَمْدُ كَمَا يَنْبَغِيْ لِجَلَالِ وَجْهِكَ وَعَظِيْمِ سُلْطَانِكَ
Translation: O my Lord, all praise is due to You as befits the Glory of Your Countenance and the Greatness of Your Authority.
Benefit Text: Praise Befitting Divine Greatness
Illustration: A small figure looking upward at vast luminous Arabic calligraphy of Allah’s name filling the sky.

Arabic: رَضِيْتُ بِاللّٰهِ رَبًّا ، وَبِالْإِسْلَامِ دِيْنًا ، وَبِمُحَمَّدٍ صَلَّى اللّٰهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ نَبِيًّا
Translation: I am pleased with Allah as my Lord, with Islam as my religion, and with Muhammad (ﷺ) as my Prophet.
Benefit Text: Pleased with Allah
Illustration: A smiling silhouette with arms gently open, soft starlight gathering quietly at the chest.

Arabic: اَللّٰهُمَّ إِنِّيْ أَسْأَلُكَ الْعَفْوَ وَالْعَافِيَةَ فِي الدُّنْيَا وَالْآخِرَةِ ، اَللّٰهُمَّ إِنِّيْ أَسْأَلُكَ الْعَفْوَ وَالْعَافِيَةَ فِيْ دِيْنِيْ وَدُنْيَايَ وَأَهْلِيْ وَمَالِيْ ، اَللّٰهُمَّ اسْتُرْ عَوْرَاتِيْ وَآمِنْ رَوْعَاتِيْ ، اَللّٰهُمَّ احْفَظْنِيْ مِنْ بَيْنِ يَدَيَّ ، وَمِنْ خَلْفِيْ ، وَعَنْ يَّمِيْنِيْ ، وَعَنْ شِمَالِيْ ، وَمِنْ فَوْقِيْ ، وَأَعُوْذُ بِعَظَمَتِكَ أَنْ أُغْتَالَ مِنْ تَحْتِيْ.
Translation: O Allah, I ask You for pardon and well-being in this world and the next. O Allah, I ask You for pardon and well-being in my religion, in my worldly affairs, in my family and in my wealth. O Allah, conceal my faults and calm my fears. O Allah, guard me from in front of me and behind me, from my right, and from my left, and from above me. I seek protection in Your Greatness from being unexpectedly destroyed from beneath me.
Benefit Text: Well-being in Both Worlds
Illustration: A soft veil of golden cloth draping gently over a kneeling figure from above.

Arabic: سُبْحَانَ اللّٰهِ وَبِحَمْدِهِ ، عَدَدَ خَلْقِهِ ، وَرِضَا نَفْسِهِ ، وَزِنَةَ عَرْشِهِ ، وَمِدَادَ كَلِمَاتِهِ.
Translation: Allah is free from imperfection and all praise is due to Him, (in ways) as numerous as all He has created, (as vast) as His pleasure, (as limitless) as the weight of His Throne, and (as endless) as the ink of His words.
Benefit Text: Outweigh All Other Dhikr
Illustration: A golden scale tipped fully to one side beneath a swirling galaxy of countless tasbih beads.

Arabic: بِسْمِ اللّٰهِ الَّذِيْ لَا يَضُرُّ مَعَ اسْمِهِ شَيْءٌ فِي الْأَرْضِ وَلَا فِي السَّمَاءِ ، وَهُوَ السَّمِيْعُ الْعَلِيْمُ.
Translation: In the Name of Allah, with whose Name nothing can harm in the earth nor in the sky. He is The All-Hearing and All-Knowing.
Benefit Text: Protect From All Harm
Illustration: A translucent dome of light surrounding a small figure, dark wisps deflecting harmlessly off its surface.

Arabic: اَللّٰهُمَّ إِنِّيْ أَعُوْذُ بِكَ أَنْ أُشْرِكَ بِكَ وَأَنَا أَعْلَمُ ، وَأَسْتَغْفِرُكَ لِمَا لَا أَعْلَمُ
Translation: O Allah, I seek refuge in You from associating anything with You knowingly, and I seek Your forgiveness for what I do not know.
Benefit Text: Seek Refuge from Shirk
Illustration: Hands cupped in repentance, soft drops of forgiving light falling from above onto the open palms.

Arabic: أَعُوْذُ بِكَلِمَاتِ اللّٰهِ التَّامَّاتِ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا خَلَقَ
Translation: I seek refuge in the perfect words of Allah from the evil of what He has created.
Benefit Text: Protection From All Evil
Illustration: A glowing circle of Arabic letters orbiting a kneeling figure, dark shadows held back at its edge.

Arabic: اَللّٰهُمَّ عَالِمَ الْغَيْبِ وَالشَّهَادَةِ ، فَاطِرَ السَّمٰوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ، رَبَّ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ وَمَلِيْكَهُ ، أَشْهَدُ أَنْ لَّا إِلٰهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ ، أَعُوْذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّ نَفْسِيْ ، وَمِنْ شَرِّ الشَّيْطَانِ وَشِرْكِهِ ، وَأَنْ أَقْتَرِفَ عَلَىٰ نَفْسِيْ سُوْءًا ، أَوْ أَجُرَّهُ إِلَىٰ مُسْلِمٍ.
Translation: O Allah, Knower of the unseen and the seen, Creator of the heavens and the earth, the Lord and Sovereign of everything; I bear witness that there is no god worthy of worship but You. I seek Your protection from the evil of my own self, from the evil of Shayṭān and from the evil of polytheism to which he calls, and from inflicting evil on myself, or bringing it upon a Muslim.
Benefit Text: Knower of the Unseen
Illustration: Two raised hands rejecting tiny crumbling idols at their feet, a clean beam of light streaming down from above.

Arabic: يَا حَيُّ يَا قَيُّوْمُ ، بِرَحْمَتِكَ أَسْتَغِيْثُ ، أَصْلِحْ لِيْ شَأْنِيْ كُلَّهُ ، وَلَا تَكِلْنِيْ إِلَىٰ نَفْسِيْ طَرْفَةَ عَيْنٍ
Translation: O Ever-Living, O Sustainer, in Your mercy I seek relief. Rectify all my affairs and do not leave me to myself even for the blink of an eye.
Benefit Text: Ya Hayyu Ya Qayyum Mustadrak Al-Hakim
Illustration: A single steady candle flame held in cupped hands, the surrounding darkness softly receding.

Arabic: اَللّٰهُمَّ أَنْتَ رَبِّيْ لَا إِلٰهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ ، خَلَقْتَنِيْ وَأَنَا عَبْدُكَ ، وَأَنَا عَلَىٰ عَهْدِكَ وَوَعْدِكَ مَا اسْتَطَعْتُ ، أَعُوْذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا صَنَعْتُ ، أَبُوْءُ لَكَ بِنِعْمَتِكَ عَلَيَّ وَأَبُوْءُ لَكَ بِذَنْبِيْ ، فَاغْفِرْ لِيْ فَإِنَّهُ لَا يَغْفِرُ الذُّنُوْبَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ.
Translation: O Allah, You are my Lord. There is no god worthy of worship except You. You have created me, and I am Your slave, and I am under Your covenant and pledge (to fulfil it) to the best of my ability. I seek Your protection from the evil that I have done. I acknowledge the favours that You have bestowed upon me, and I admit my sins. Forgive me, for none forgives sins but You.
Benefit Text: Sayyid al-Istighfar
Illustration: Two tall gates of Paradise swinging slowly open, warm golden light pouring through onto a kneeling figure with head bowed.

Arabic: اَللّٰهُمَّ إِنِّيْ أَصْبَحْتُ أُشْهِدُكَ ، وَأُشْهِدُ حَمَلَةَ عَرْشِكَ وَمَلَائِكَتَكَ وَجَمِيْعَ خَلْقِكَ ، أَنَّكَ أَنْتَ اللّٰهُ لَا إِلٰهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ وَحْدَكَ لَا شَرِيْكَ لَكَ ، وَأَنَّ مُحَمَّدًا عَبْدُكَ وَرَسُوْلُكَ.
Translation: O Allah, I have entered the morning, and I call upon You, the bearers of Your Throne, Your angels and all creation, to bear witness that surely You are Allah. There is no god worthy of worship except You Alone. You have no partners, and that Muḥammad ﷺ is Your slave and Your Messenger.
Benefit Text: Freed from the Hellfire
Illustration: A figure with arms open, four angelic light-figures circling, a closing door of fire fading away below.

Arabic: اَللّٰهُمَّ عَافِنِيْ فِيْ بَدَنِيْ ، اَللّٰهُمَّ عَافِنِيْ فِيْ سَمْعِيْ ، اَللّٰهُمَّ عَافِنِيْ فِيْ بَصَرِيْ ، لَا إِلٰهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ ، اَللّٰهُمَّ إِنِّيْ أَعُوْذُ بِكَ مِنَ الْكُفْرِ وَالْفَقْرِ، وأَعُوْذُ بِكَ مِنْ عَذَابِ الْقَبْرِ، لَا إِلٰهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ.
Translation: O Allah, grant me well-being in my body. O Allah, grant me well-being in my hearing. O Allah, grant me well-being in my sight. There is no god worthy of worship except You. O Allah, I seek Your protection from disbelief and poverty and I seek Your protection from the punishment of the grave. There is no god worthy of worship except You.
Benefit Text: Good Health & Protection
Illustration: A simple white-shrouded form at peace under a soft starlit sky, a gentle column of mercy-light descending from above.

Arabic: حَسْبِيَ اللّٰهُ لَا إِلٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ، عَلَيْهِ تَوَكَّلْتُ ، وَهُوَ رَبُّ الْعَرْشِ الْعَظِيْمِ.
Translation: Allah is sufficient for me. There is no god worthy of worship except Him. I have placed my trust in Him only and He is the Lord of the Magnificent Throne.
Benefit Text: Allah Will Suffice You
Illustration: Pale gold sunrise over a calm horizon, two hands gently raised in supplication toward the dawn light.

Arabic: أَصْبَحْنَا وَأَصْبَحَ الْمُلْكُ لِلّٰهِ رَبِّ الْعَالَمِيْنَ ، اَللّٰهُمَّ إِنِّيْ أَسْأَلُكَ خَيْرَ هٰذَا الْيَوْمِ ، فَتْحَهُ وَنَصْرَهُ وَنُوْرَهُ وَبَرَكَتَهُ وَهُدَاهُ ، وَأَعُوْذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا فِيْهِ وَشَرِّ مَا بَعْدَهُ.
Translation: We have entered the morning and at this very time the whole kingdom belongs to Allah, Lord of the Worlds. O Allah, I ask You for the goodness of this day: its victory, its help, its light, and its blessings and guidance. I seek Your protection from the evil that is in it and from the evil that follows it.
Benefit Text: Ask Allah to Bless Your Day
Illustration: A glowing key of dominion suspended in a starlit sky above an open landscape of mountains, oceans, and earth.

Arabic: لَا إِلٰهَ إِلَّا اللّٰهُ وَحْدَهُ لَا شَرِيْكَ لَهُ ، لَهُ الْمُلْكُ وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ ، وَهُوَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيْرٌ.
Translation: There is no god worthy of worship except Allah. He is Alone and He has no partner whatsoever. To Him Alone belong all sovereignty and all praise. He is over all things All-Powerful.
Benefit Text: An Unparalleled Reward
Illustration: Ten chains breaking and falling from a freed wrist, a white dove rising into warm dawn light.

Arabic: سُبْحَانَ اللّٰهِ وَبِحَمْدِهِ.
Translation: Allah is free from imperfection, and all praise is due to Him.
Benefit Text: Get Your Sins Forgiven
Illustration: Pale gold sunrise over a calm horizon, two hands gently raised in supplication toward the dawn light.

Arabic: بِسْمِ اللّٰهِ الرَّحْمٰنِ الرَّحِيْمِ اَلْحَمْدُ لِلّٰهِ رَبِّ الْعَالَمِيْنَ ، الرَّحْمٰنِ الرَّحِيْمِ ، مَالِكِ يَوْمِ الدِّيْنِ ، إِيَّاكَ نَعْبُدُ وَإِيَّاكَ نَسْتَعِيْنُ ، اِهْدِنَا الصِّرَاطَ الْمُسْتَقِيْمَ ، صِرَاطَ الَّذِيْنَ أَنْعَمْتَ عَلَيْهِمْ غَيْرِ الْمَغْضُوْبِ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلَا الضَّالِّيْنَ
Translation: In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful. All praise is due to Allah, Lord of the worlds. The Most Gracious, the Most Merciful. Master of the Day of Judgment. You alone we worship, and You alone we ask for help. Guide us to the straight path — the path of those upon whom You have bestowed favor, not of those who have evoked Your anger or of those who are astray.
Benefit Text: Al Fateha
Illustration: Open Quran turned to Surah Al-Fatihah, gentle light radiating outward from the calligraphy onto open palms.

Arabic: الٓمٓ ، ذٰلِكَ الْكِتَابُ لَا رَيْبَ فِيْهِ هُدًى لِّلْمُتَّقِيْنَ ، الَّذِيْنَ يُؤْمِنُوْنَ بِالْغَيْبِ وَيُقِيْمُوْنَ الصَّلَاةَ وَمِمَّا رَزَقْنَاهُمْ يُنْفِقُوْنَ ، وَالَّذِيْنَ يُؤْمِنُوْنَ بِمَا أُنْزِلَ إِلَيْكَ وَمَا أُنْزِلَ مِنْ قَبْلِكَ وَبِالْآخِرَةِ هُمْ يُوْقِنُوْنَ ، أُولٰئِكَ عَلَىٰ هُدًى مِنْ رَبِّهِمْ وَأُولٰئِكَ هُمُ الْمُفْلِحُوْنَ
Translation: Alif, Lam, Mim. This is the Book about which there is no doubt, a guidance for those conscious of Allah — who believe in the unseen, establish prayer, and spend out of what We have provided for them, and who believe in what has been revealed to you and what was revealed before you, and of the Hereafter they are certain. Those are upon guidance from their Lord, and it is those who are the successful.
Benefit Text: Alif Laam Meem Ad-Darimi
Illustration: Open Quran with the opening of Surah Al-Baqarah glowing, soft path of guidance light leading into the page.

Arabic: أَعُوْذُ بِاللّٰهِ مِنَ الشَّيْطَانِ الرَّجِيْمِ. اَللّٰهُ لَآ إِلٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ الْحَىُّ الْقَيُّوْمُ ، لَا تَأْخُذُهُۥ سِنَةٌ وَّلَا نَوْمٌ ، لَهُ مَا فِى السَّمٰـوٰتِ وَمَا فِى الْأَرْضِ ، مَنْ ذَا الَّذِىْ يَشْفَعُ عِنْدَهُ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِهِۦ ، يَعْلَمُ مَا بَيْنَ أَيْدِيْهِمْ وَمَا خَلْفَهُمْ ، وَلَا يُحِيْطُوْنَ بِشَىْءٍ مِّنْ عِلْمِهِٓ إِلَّا بِمَا شَآءَ ، وَسِعَ كُرْسِيُّهُ السَّمٰـوٰتِ وَالْأَرْضَ، وَلَا يَئُوْدُهُۥ حِفْظُهُمَا ، وَهُوَ الْعَلِىُّ الْعَظِيْمُ.
Translation: I seek the protection of Allah from the accursed Shayṭān. Allah, there is no god worthy of worship but He, the Ever Living, The Sustainer of all. Neither drowsiness overtakes Him nor sleep. To Him Alone belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. Who is it that can intercede with Him except with His permission? He knows what is before them and what will be after them, and they encompass not a thing of His knowledge except for what He wills. His Kursī extends over the heavens and the earth, and their preservation does not tire Him. And He is the Most High, the Magnificent. (2:255)
Benefit Text: Ayatul Kursi
Illustration: Glowing celestial throne extending across the heavens and earth, with a soft golden dome of protection forming over a kneeling worshipper.

Arabic: لَا إِكْرَاهَ فِي الدِّيْنِ ، قَدْ تَبَيَّنَ الرُّشْدُ مِنَ الْغَيِّ ، فَمَنْ يَّكْفُرْ بِالطَّاغُوْتِ وَيُؤْمِنْ بِاللّٰهِ فَقَدِ اسْتَمْسَكَ بِالْعُرْوَةِ الْوُثْقَىٰ لَا انْفِصَامَ لَهَا ، وَاللّٰهُ سَمِيْعٌ عَلِيْمٌ
Translation: There shall be no compulsion in the religion. The right course has become clear from the wrong. So whoever disbelieves in false deities and believes in Allah has grasped the most trustworthy handhold with no break in it. And Allah is Hearing and Knowing.
Benefit Text: There are no specific conditions or requirements to recite this Quranic ayah as part of the morning or evening adhkar. However, this ayah is included in Ruqya and can be added as a supplementary recitation.
Illustration: Two open palms gently raised above a softly glowing chest, soft blue healing light flowing from the hands into the heart.

Arabic: اَللّٰهُ وَلِيُّ الَّذِيْنَ آمَنُوْا يُخْرِجُهُمْ مِّنَ الظُّلُمَاتِ إِلَى النُّوْرِ ، وَالَّذِيْنَ كَفَرُوْا أَوْلِيَاؤُهُمُ الطَّاغُوْتُ يُخْرِجُوْنَهُمْ مِّنَ النُّوْرِ إِلَى الظُّلُمَاتِ ، أُولٰئِكَ أَصْحَابُ النَّارِ هُمْ فِيْهَا خَالِدُوْنَ
Translation: Allah is the ally of those who believe. He brings them out from darknesses into the light. And those who disbelieve — their allies are false deities. They take them out of the light into darknesses. Those are the companions of the Fire; they will abide eternally therein.
Benefit Text: There are no specific conditions or requirements to recite this Quranic ayah as part of the morning or evening adhkar. However, this ayah is included in Ruqya and can be added as a supplementary recitation.
Illustration: Two open palms gently raised above a softly glowing chest, soft blue healing light flowing from the hands into the heart.

Arabic: لِلّٰهِ مَا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَمَا فِي الْأَرْضِ ، وَإِنْ تُبْدُوْا مَا فِيْ أَنْفُسِكُمْ أَوْ تُخْفُوْهُ يُحَاسِبْكُمْ بِهِ اللّٰهُ ، فَيَغْفِرُ لِمَنْ يَّشَاءُ وَيُعَذِّبُ مَنْ يَّشَاءُ ، وَاللّٰهُ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيْرٌ
Translation: To Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is in the earth. Whether you show what is within yourselves or conceal it, Allah will bring you to account for it. Then He will forgive whom He wills and punish whom He wills, and Allah is over all things competent.
Benefit Text: To Allah Belongs All
Illustration: A glowing key of dominion suspended in a starlit sky above an open landscape of mountains, oceans, and earth.

Arabic: آمَنَ الرَّسُوْلُ بِمَا أُنْزِلَ إِلَيْهِ مِنْ رَّبِّهِ وَالْمُؤْمِنُوْنَ ، كُلٌّ آمَنَ بِاللّٰهِ وَمَلَائِكَتِهِ وَكُتُبِهِ وَرُسُلِهِ لَا نُفَرِّقُ بَيْنَ أَحَدٍ مِّنْ رُسُلِهِ ، وَقَالُوْا سَمِعْنَا وَأَطَعْنَا غُفْرَانَكَ رَبَّنَا وَإِلَيْكَ الْمَصِيْرُ
Translation: The Messenger has believed in what was revealed to him from his Lord, and so have the believers. All of them have believed in Allah and His angels and His books and His messengers, saying, "We make no distinction between any of His messengers." And they say, "We hear and we obey. Grant us Your forgiveness, our Lord, and to You is the final destination."
Benefit Text: The Messenger Has Believed
Illustration: Cupped hands holding a soft, weighted light of guidance, an unburdened figure standing upright beside.

Arabic: لَا يُكَلِّفُ اللّٰهُ نَفْسًا إِلَّا وُسْعَهَا ، لَهَا مَا كَسَبَتْ وَعَلَيْهَا مَا اكْتَسَبَتْ ، رَبَّنَا لَا تُؤَاخِذْنَا إِنْ نَسِيْنَا أَوْ أَخْطَأْنَا ، رَبَّنَا وَلَا تَحْمِلْ عَلَيْنَا إِصْرًا كَمَا حَمَلْتَهُ عَلَى الَّذِيْنَ مِنْ قَبْلِنَا ، رَبَّنَا وَلَا تُحَمِّلْنَا مَا لَا طَاقَةَ لَنَا بِهِ ، وَاعْفُ عَنَّا وَاغْفِرْ لَنَا وَارْحَمْنَا ، أَنْتَ مَوْلَانَا فَانْصُرْنَا عَلَى الْقَوْمِ الْكَافِرِيْنَ
Translation: Allah does not burden a soul beyond that it can bear. It will have the consequence of what good it has gained, and it will bear the consequence of what evil it has earned. "Our Lord, do not impose blame upon us if we have forgotten or erred. Our Lord, and lay not upon us a burden like that which You laid upon those before us. Our Lord, and burden us not with that which we have no ability to bear. And pardon us; and forgive us; and have mercy upon us. You are our protector, so give us victory over the disbelieving people."
Benefit Text: Allah Does Not Burden a Soul
Illustration: Cupped hands holding a soft, weighted light of guidance, an unburdened figure standing upright beside.

Arabic: بِسْمِ اللّٰهِ الرَّحْمٰنِ الرَّحِيْمِ قُلْ هُوَ اللّٰهُ أَحَدٌ ، اَللّٰهُ الصَّمَدُ ، لَمْ يَلِدْ وَلَمْ يُوْلَدْ ، وَلَمْ يَكُنْ لَّهُ كُفُوًا أَحَدٌ
Translation: In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful. Say, "He is Allah, the One. Allah, the Eternal Refuge. He neither begets nor is born, nor is there to Him any equivalent."
Benefit Text: Surah Al-Ikhlas
Illustration: Open Quran turned to Surah Al-Ikhlas, calligraphy glowing, a single soft ray of light rising from the page.

Arabic: بِسْمِ اللّٰهِ الرَّحْمٰنِ الرَّحِيْمِ قُلْ أَعُوْذُ بِرَبِّ الْفَلَقِ ، مِنْ شَرِّ مَا خَلَقَ ، وَمِنْ شَرِّ غَاسِقٍ إِذَا وَقَبَ ، وَمِنْ شَرِّ النَّفَّاثَاتِ فِي الْعُقَدِ ، وَمِنْ شَرِّ حَاسِدٍ إِذَا حَسَدَ
Translation: In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful. Say, "I seek refuge in the Lord of daybreak, from the evil of that which He created, and from the evil of darkness when it settles, and from the evil of the blowers in knots, and from the evil of an envier when he envies."
Benefit Text: Surah Al-Falaq
Illustration: Open Quran on Surah Al-Falaq, dawn breaking on the horizon, a dark knot of evil dissolving into the morning light.

Arabic: بِسْمِ اللّٰهِ الرَّحْمٰنِ الرَّحِيْمِ قُلْ أَعُوْذُ بِرَبِّ النَّاسِ ، مَلِكِ النَّاسِ ، إِلٰهِ النَّاسِ ، مِنْ شَرِّ الْوَسْوَاسِ الْخَنَّاسِ ، الَّذِيْ يُوَسْوِسُ فِيْ صُدُوْرِ النَّاسِ ، مِنَ الْجِنَّةِ وَالنَّاسِ
Translation: In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful. Say, "I seek refuge in the Lord of mankind, the Sovereign of mankind, the God of mankind, from the evil of the retreating whisperer — who whispers in the breasts of mankind — from among the jinn and mankind."
Benefit Text: Surah An-Nas
Illustration: Open Quran on Surah An-Nas, a whispering shadow retreating from a softly glowing heart-silhouette.

Arabic: أَمْسَيْنَا وَأَمْسَى الْمُلْكُ لِلّٰهِ وَالْحَمْدُ لِلّٰهِ ، لَا إِلٰهَ إِلَّا اللّٰهُ وَحْدَهُ لَا شَرِيْكَ لَهُ ، لَهُ الْمُلْكُ وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ ، وَهُوَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيْرٌ ، رَبِّ أَسْأَلُكَ خَيْرَ مَا فِيْ هٰذِهِ اللَّيْلَةِ وَخَيْرَ مَا بَعْدَهَا ، وَأَعُوْذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا فِيْ هٰذِهِ اللَّيْلَةِ وَشَرِّ مَا بَعْدَهَا ، رَبِّ أَعُوْذُ بِكَ مِنَ الْكَسَلِ وَسُوْءِ الْكِبَرِ ، رَبِّ أَعُوْذُ بِكَ مِنْ عَذَابٍ فِي النَّارِ وَعَذَابٍ فِي الْقَبْرِ.
Translation: We have entered the evening and at this very time the whole kingdom belongs to Allah. All praise is due to Allah. There is no god worthy of worship except Allah, the One; He has no partner with Him. The entire kingdom belongs solely to Him, to Him is all praise due, and He is All-Powerful over everything. My Lord, I ask You for the good that is in this night and the good that follows it, and I seek Your protection from the evil that is in this night and from the evil that follows it. My Lord, I seek Your protection from laziness and the misery of old age. My Lord, I seek Your protection from the torment of the Hell-fire and the punishment of the grave.
Benefit Text: Dominion Belongs to Allah
Illustration: A simple white-shrouded form at peace under a soft starlit sky, a gentle column of mercy-light descending from above.

Arabic: أَمْسَيْنَا عَلَىٰ فِطْرَةِ الْإِسْلَامِ ، وَعَلَىٰ كَلِمَةِ الْإِخْلَاصِ ، وَعَلَىٰ دِيْنِ نَبِيِّنَا مُحَمَّدٍ ، وَعَلَىٰ مِلَّةِ أَبِيْنَا إِبْرَاهِيْمَ حَنِيْفًا مُّسْلِمًا وَّمَا كَانَ مِنَ الْمُشْرِكِيْنَ.
Translation: We have entered the evening upon the natural religion of Islam, the statement of pure faith (i.e. Shahādah), the religion of our Prophet Muhammad ﷺ and upon the way of our father Ibrāhīm, who turned away from all that is false, having surrendered to Allah, and he was not of the polytheists.
Benefit Text: Upon the Fitrah of Islam
Illustration: A small child-silhouette with a soft halo, surrounded by five gentle stars representing the pillars of Islam.

Arabic: اَللّٰهُمَّ بِكَ أَمْسَيْنَا وَبِكَ أَصْبَحْنَا وَبِكَ نَحْيَا وَبِكَ نَمُوْتُ وَإِلَيْكَ الْمَصِيْرُ.
Translation: O Allah, by You we have entered the evening and by You we enter upon the morning. By You, we live and we die, and to You is the return.
Benefit Text: By Your Leave
Illustration: Sunrise and sunset side by side on a continuous horizon, a soft path of light connecting the two.

Arabic: اَللّٰهُمَّ إِنِّيْ أَمْسَيْتُ مِنْكَ فِيْ نِعْمَةٍ وَعَافِيَةٍ وَسِتْرٍ ، فَأَتِمَّ عَلَيَّ نِعْمَتَكَ وَعَافِيَتَكَ وَسِتْرَكَ فِي الدُّنْيَا وَالْآخِرَةِ
Translation: O Allah, I have entered the evening receiving from You blessings, well-being and concealment of my faults. So complete Your blessings upon me, Your well-being, and Your concealment in this world and the Hereafter.
Benefit Text: Blessings, Well-being and Concealment
Illustration: Pink and white rose petals drifting upward toward elegant calligraphy of the Prophet’s name, soft gold halo around it.

Arabic: اَللّٰهُمَّ مَا أَمْسَىٰ بِيْ مِنْ نِّعْمَةٍ أَوْ بِأَحَدٍ مِّنْ خَلْقِكَ ، فَمِنْكَ وَحْدَكَ لَا شَرِيْكَ لَكَ ، فَلَكَ الْحَمْدُ وَلَكَ الشُّكْرُ.
Translation: O Allah, all the favours that I or anyone from Your creation has received in the evening, are from You Alone. You have no partner. To You Alone belong all praise and all thanks.
Benefit Text: Fulfil Obligation of Gratitude
Illustration: Hands cupped to receive rays of light from above, tiny green seedlings sprouting from the palms.

Arabic: يَا رَبِّ لَكَ الْحَمْدُ كَمَا يَنْبَغِيْ لِجَلَالِ وَجْهِكَ وَعَظِيْمِ سُلْطَانِكَ
Translation: O my Lord, all praise is due to You as befits the Glory of Your Countenance and the Greatness of Your Authority.
Benefit Text: Praise Befitting Divine Greatness
Illustration: A small figure looking upward at vast luminous Arabic calligraphy of Allah’s name filling the sky.

Arabic: رَضِيْتُ بِاللّٰهِ رَبًّا ، وَبِالْإِسْلَامِ دِيْنًا ، وَبِمُحَمَّدٍ نَّبِيًّا.
Translation: I am pleased with Allah as my Lord, with Islām as my religion and with Muhammad ﷺ as my Prophet.
Benefit Text: Pleased with Allah
Illustration: A smiling silhouette with arms gently open, soft starlight gathering quietly at the chest.

Arabic: اَللّٰهُمَّ إِنِّيْ أَسْأَلُكَ الْعَافِيَةَ فِي الدُّنْيَا وَالْآخِرَةِ ، اَللّٰهُمَّ إِنِّيْ أَسْأَلُكَ الْعَفْوَ وَالْعَافِيَةَ فِيْ دِيْنِيْ وَدُنْيَايَ وَأَهْلِيْ وَمَالِيْ ، اَللّٰهُمَّ اسْتُرْ عَوْرَاتِيْ وَآمِنْ رَوْعَاتِيْ ، اَللّٰهُمَّ احْفَظْنِيْ مِنْ بَيْنِ يَدَيَّ ، وَمِنْ خَلْفِيْ ، وَعَنْ يَّمِيْنِيْ ، وَعَنْ شِمَالِيْ ، وَمِنْ فَوْقِيْ ، وَأَعُوْذُ بِعَظَمَتِكَ أَنْ أُغْتَالَ مِنْ تَحْتِيْ.
Translation: O Allah, I ask You for well-being in this world and the next. O Allah, I ask You for forgiveness and well-being in my religion, in my worldly affairs, in my family and in my wealth. O Allah, conceal my faults and calm my fears. O Allah, guard me from in front of me and behind me, from my right, and from my left, and from above me. I seek protection in Your Greatness from being unexpectedly destroyed from beneath me.
Benefit Text: Well-being in Both Worlds
Illustration: A soft veil of golden cloth draping gently over a kneeling figure from above.

Arabic: سُبْحَانَ اللّٰهِ وَبِحَمْدِهِ ، عَدَدَ خَلْقِهِ ، وَرِضَا نَفْسِهِ ، وَزِنَةَ عَرْشِهِ ، وَمِدَادَ كَلِمَاتِهِ
Translation: Allah is free from imperfection and all praise is due to Him, as numerous as all He has created, as vast as His pleasure, as limitless as the weight of His Throne, and as endless as the ink of His words.
Benefit Text: Outweigh All Other Dhikr
Illustration: A golden scale tipped fully to one side beneath a swirling galaxy of countless tasbih beads.

Arabic: بِسْمِ اللّٰهِ الَّذِيْ لَا يَضُرُّ مَعَ اسْمِهِ شَيْءٌ فِي الْأَرْضِ وَلَا فِي السَّمَاءِ ، وَهُوَ السَّمِيْعُ الْعَلِيْمُ.
Translation: In the Name of Allah, with whose Name nothing can harm in the earth nor in the sky. He is The All-Hearing and All-Knowing.
Benefit Text: Protect From All Harm
Illustration: A translucent dome of light surrounding a small figure, dark wisps deflecting harmlessly off its surface.

Arabic: اَللّٰهُمَّ إِنِّيْ أَعُوْذُ بِكَ أَنْ أُشْرِكَ بِكَ وَأَنَا أَعْلَمُ ، وَأَسْتَغْفِرُكَ لِمَا لَا أَعْلَمُ
Translation: O Allah, I seek refuge in You from associating anything with You knowingly, and I seek Your forgiveness for what I do not know.
Benefit Text: Seek Refuge from Shirk
Illustration: Hands cupped in repentance, soft drops of forgiving light falling from above onto the open palms.

Arabic: أَعُوْذُ بِكَلِمَاتِ اللّٰهِ التَّامَّاتِ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا خَلَقَ.
Translation: I seek protection in Allah's perfect words from the evil of whatever He has created.
Benefit Text: Protection From All Evil
Illustration: A glowing circle of Arabic letters orbiting a kneeling figure, dark shadows held back at its edge.

Arabic: اَللّٰهُمَّ عَالِمَ الْغَيْبِ وَالشَّهَادَةِ ، فَاطِرَ السَّمٰوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ، رَبَّ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ وَمَلِيْكَهُ ، أَشْهَدُ أَنْ لَّا إِلٰهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ ، أَعُوْذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّ نَفْسِيْ ، وَمِنْ شَرِّ الشَّيْطَانِ وَشِرْكِهِ ، وَأَنْ أَقْتَرِفَ عَلَىٰ نَفْسِيْ سُوْءًا ، أَوْ أَجُرَّهُ إِلَىٰ مُسْلِمٍ.
Translation: O Allah, Knower of the unseen and the seen, Creator of the heavens and the earth, the Lord and Sovereign of everything; I bear witness that there is no god worthy of worship but You. I seek Your protection from the evil of my own self, from the evil of Shayṭān and from the evil of polytheism to which he calls, and from inflicting evil on myself, or bringing it upon a Muslim.
Benefit Text: Knower of the Unseen
Illustration: Two raised hands rejecting tiny crumbling idols at their feet, a clean beam of light streaming down from above.

Arabic: يَا حَيُّ يَا قَيُّوْمُ ، بِرَحْمَتِكَ أَسْتَغِيْثُ ، أَصْلِحْ لِيْ شَأْنِيْ كُلَّهُ ، وَلَا تَكِلْنِيْ إِلَىٰ نَفْسِيْ طَرْفَةَ عَيْنٍ.
Translation: O The Ever Living, The Sustainer of all; I seek assistance through Your mercy. Rectify all of my affairs and do not entrust me to myself for the blink of an eye.
Benefit Text: Ya Hayyu Ya Qayyum Mustadrak Al-Hakim
Illustration: A single steady candle flame held in cupped hands, the surrounding darkness softly receding.

Arabic: اَللّٰهُمَّ أَنْتَ رَبِّيْ لَا إِلٰهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ ، خَلَقْتَنِيْ وَأَنَا عَبْدُكَ ، وَأَنَا عَلَىٰ عَهْدِكَ وَوَعْدِكَ مَا اسْتَطَعْتُ ، أَعُوْذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا صَنَعْتُ ، أَبُوْءُ لَكَ بِنِعْمَتِكَ عَلَيَّ وَأَبُوْءُ لَكَ بِذَنْبِيْ ، فَاغْفِرْ لِيْ فَإِنَّهُ لَا يَغْفِرُ الذُّنُوْبَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ.
Translation: O Allah, You are my Lord. There is no god worthy of worship except You. You have created me, and I am Your slave, and I am under Your covenant and pledge (to fulfil it) to the best of my ability. I seek Your protection from the evil that I have done. I acknowledge the favours that You have bestowed upon me, and I admit my sins. Forgive me, for none forgives sins but You.
Benefit Text: Sayyid al-Istighfar
Illustration: Two tall gates of Paradise swinging slowly open, warm golden light pouring through onto a kneeling figure with head bowed.

Arabic: اَللّٰهُمَّ إِنِّيْ أَمْسَيْتُ أُشْهِدُكَ وَأُشْهِدُ حَمَلَةَ عَرْشِكَ وَمَلَائِكَتَكَ وَجَمِيْعَ خَلْقِكَ ، أَنَّكَ أَنْتَ اللّٰهُ لَا إِلٰهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ وَحْدَكَ لَا شَرِيْكَ لَكَ ، وَأَنَّ مُحَمَّدًا عَبْدُكَ وَرَسُوْلُكَ.
Translation: O Allah, I have entered the evening, and I call upon You, the bearers of Your Throne, Your angels and all creation, to bear witness that surely You are Allah. There is no god worthy of worship except You Alone. You have no partners, and that Muḥammad ﷺ is Your slave and Your Messenger.
Benefit Text: Freed from the Hellfire
Illustration: A figure with arms open, four angelic light-figures circling, a closing door of fire fading away below.

Arabic: اَللّٰهُمَّ عَافِنِيْ فِيْ بَدَنِيْ ، اَللّٰهُمَّ عَافِنِيْ فِيْ سَمْعِيْ ، اَللّٰهُمَّ عَافِنِيْ فِيْ بَصَرِيْ ، لَا إِلٰهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ ، اَللّٰهُمَّ إِنِّيْ أَعُوْذُ بِكَ مِنَ الْكُفْرِ وَالْفَقْرِ، وأَعُوْذُ بِكَ مِنْ عَذَابِ الْقَبْرِ، لَا إِلٰهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ.
Translation: O Allah, grant me well-being in my body. O Allah, grant me well-being in my hearing. O Allah, grant me well-being in my sight. There is no god worthy of worship except You. O Allah, I seek Your protection from disbelief and poverty and I seek Your protection from the punishment of the grave. There is no god worthy of worship except You.
Benefit Text: Good Health & Protection
Illustration: A simple white-shrouded form at peace under a soft starlit sky, a gentle column of mercy-light descending from above.

Arabic: حَسْبِيَ اللّٰهُ لَا إِلٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ، عَلَيْهِ تَوَكَّلْتُ ، وَهُوَ رَبُّ الْعَرْشِ الْعَظِيْمِ.
Translation: Allah is sufficient for me. There is no god worthy of worship except Him. I have placed my trust in Him only and He is the Lord of the Magnificent Throne.
Benefit Text: Allah Will Suffice You
Illustration: Soft purple and orange dusk over a mosque silhouette, hands raised in supplication beneath the twilight sky.

Arabic: لَا إِلٰهَ إِلَّا اللّٰهُ وَحْدَهُ لَا شَرِيْكَ لَهُ ، لَهُ الْمُلْكُ وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ ، وَهُوَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيْرٌ.
Translation: There is no god worthy of worship except Allah. He is Alone and He has no partner whatsoever. To Him Alone belong all sovereignty and all praise. He is over all things All-Powerful.
Benefit Text: An Unparalleled Reward
Illustration: Ten chains breaking and falling from a freed wrist, a white dove rising into warm dawn light.

Arabic: سُبْحَانَ اللّٰهِ وَبِحَمْدِهِ.
Translation: Allah is free from imperfection, and all praise is due to Him.
Benefit Text: Get Your Sins Forgiven
Illustration: Soft purple and orange dusk over a mosque silhouette, hands raised in supplication beneath the twilight sky.

Arabic: اَللّٰهُمَّ صَلِّ عَلَىٰ مُحَمَّدٍ وَّعَلَىٰ اٰلِ مُحَمَّدٍ ، كَمَا صَلَّيْتَ عَلَىٰ إِبْرَاهِيْمَ وَعَلَىٰ اٰلِ إِبْرَاهِيْمَ ، إِنَّكَ حَمِيْدٌ مَّجِيْدٌ ، اَللّٰهُمَّ بَارِكْ عَلَىٰ مُحَمَّدٍ وَّعَلَىٰ اٰلِ مُحَمَّدٍ ، كَمَا بَارَكْتَ عَلَىٰ إِبْرَاهِيْمَ وَعَلَىٰ اٰلِ إِبْرَاهِيْمَ ، إِنَّكَ حَمِيْدٌ مَّجِيْدٌ.
Translation: O Allah, honour and have mercy upon Muhammad and the family of Muhammad as You have honoured and had mercy upon Ibrāhīm and the family of Ibrāhīm. Indeed, You are the Most Praiseworthy, the Most Glorious. O Allah, bless Muhammad and the family of Muhammad as You have blessed Ibrāhīm and the family of Ibrāhīm. Indeed, You are the Most Praiseworthy, the Most Glorious.
Benefit Text: Durood Ibrahim
Illustration: Pink and white rose petals drifting upward toward elegant calligraphy of the Prophet’s name, soft gold halo around it.

Arabic: سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ
Translation: Glory be to Allah
Benefit Text: After every obligatory prayer: 33 SubhanAllah, 33 Alhamdulillah, 33 Allahu Akbar — sins forgiven even if like the foam of the sea.
Illustration: Hands in seated tashahhud holding wooden prayer beads, soft golden glow rising from the fingertips.

Arabic: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ
Translation: All praise is for Allah
Benefit Text: Said 33 times after every obligatory prayer. Fills the scales on the Day of Judgment.
Illustration: Hands in seated tashahhud holding wooden prayer beads, soft golden glow rising from the fingertips.

Arabic: اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ
Translation: Allah is the Greatest
Benefit Text: Said 33 times after every obligatory prayer. Together with the tasbihat completes 100 remembrances.
Illustration: Hands in seated tashahhud holding wooden prayer beads, soft golden glow rising from the fingertips.

Arabic: اللَّهُ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا هُوَ الْحَيُّ الْقَيُّومُ
Translation: Allah — there is no deity except Him, the Ever-Living, the Sustainer
Benefit Text: Whoever recites Ayat al-Kursi after every obligatory prayer is guaranteed Paradise when they die.
Illustration: Glowing celestial throne extending across the heavens and earth, with a soft golden dome of protection forming over a kneeling worshipper.

Arabic: لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لَا شَرِيكَ لَهُ لَهُ الْمُلْكُ وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ وَهُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ
Translation: None has the right to be worshipped but Allah alone, no partner has He. His is the dominion and the praise, and He has power over all things
Benefit Text: Whoever says this after every obligatory prayer — his sins are forgiven even if they are like the foam of the sea.
Illustration: Hands in seated tashahhud holding wooden prayer beads, soft golden glow rising from the fingertips.

Arabic: أَسْتَغْفِرُ اللَّهَ
Translation: I seek forgiveness from Allah
Benefit Text: The Prophet used to say Astaghfirullah 3 times after every prayer.
Illustration: Hands in seated tashahhud holding wooden prayer beads, soft golden glow rising from the fingertips.

Arabic: اللَّهُ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا هُوَ الْحَيُّ الْقَيُّومُ
Translation: Allah — there is no deity except Him, the Ever-Living, the Sustainer
Benefit Text: Recite Ayat al-Kursi before sleep — a guardian from Allah will protect you, Shaytan will not come near until morning.
Illustration: Glowing celestial throne extending across the heavens and earth, with a soft golden dome of protection forming over a kneeling worshipper.

Arabic: سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ
Translation: Glory be to Allah
Benefit Text: Before sleeping: 33 SubhanAllah, 33 Alhamdulillah, 34 Allahu Akbar — this is better for you than a servant.
Illustration: A sleeping figure beneath a soft blanket, a crescent moon and stars above, a gentle guardian-light hovering nearby.

Arabic: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ
Translation: All praise is for Allah
Benefit Text: Part of the bedtime tasbih prescribed by the Prophet to Fatimah and Ali.
Illustration: A sleeping figure beneath a soft blanket, a crescent moon and stars above, a gentle guardian-light hovering nearby.

Arabic: اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ
Translation: Allah is the Greatest
Benefit Text: Part of the bedtime tasbih — better for you than having a servant.
Illustration: A sleeping figure beneath a soft blanket, a crescent moon and stars above, a gentle guardian-light hovering nearby.

Arabic: بِسْمِكَ اللَّهُمَّ أَمُوتُ وَأَحْيَا
Translation: In Your name, O Allah, I die and I live
Benefit Text: The Prophet used to say this every night before sleeping.
Illustration: Sleeping figure under a crescent moon on one side, the same figure rising in soft dawn light on the other side.

Arabic: تَبَارَكَ الَّذِي بِيَدِهِ الْمُلْكُ
Translation: Blessed is He in Whose hand is the dominion (Surah Al-Mulk)
Benefit Text: Surah Al-Mulk intercedes for its reciter and protects from the punishment of the grave.
Illustration: Open Quran on Surah Al-Mulk under a starry night sky, a soft shield of light enveloping a sleeping form.

Arabic: قُلْ هُوَ اللَّهُ أَحَدٌ
Translation: Say: He is Allah, the One (Surah Al-Ikhlas — 3 times)
Benefit Text: Reciting Al-Ikhlas 3x before sleeping equals reciting the entire Quran.
Illustration: Open Quran turned to Surah Al-Ikhlas, calligraphy glowing, a single soft ray of light rising from the page.

Arabic: رَبَّنَا لَا تُزِغْ قُلُوبَنَا بَعْدَ إِذْ هَدَيْتَنَا وَهَبْ لَنَا مِن لَّدُنكَ رَحْمَةً
Translation: Our Lord, let not our hearts deviate after You have guided us and grant us from Yourself mercy
Benefit Text: Dua for steadfastness in faith — essential for the Ummah.
Illustration: Hands cupped gently around a small green globe, a soft halo of light spreading over the Muslim lands.

Arabic: رَبَّنَا أَفْرِغْ عَلَيْنَا صَبْرًا وَثَبِّتْ أَقْدَامَنَا وَانصُرْنَا عَلَى الْقَوْمِ الْكَافِرِينَ
Translation: Our Lord, pour upon us patience, plant firmly our feet, and give us victory over the disbelieving people
Benefit Text: Dua of the believers before battle — for the victory of the Ummah.
Illustration: Hands cupped gently around a small green globe, a soft halo of light spreading over the Muslim lands.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ انْصُرِ الْمُسْتَضْعَفِينَ مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ
Translation: O Allah, grant victory to the weak and oppressed among the believers
Benefit Text: Dua for the oppressed Muslims globally — make it a daily habit.
Illustration: Hands cupped gently around a small green globe, a soft halo of light spreading over the Muslim lands.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ لَكَ الْحَمْدُ أَنْتَ نُورُ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ
Translation: O Allah, to You belongs all praise. You are the light of the heavens and the earth
Benefit Text: The Prophet used to begin Tahajjud with this powerful du'a.
Illustration: Silhouette in sajdah on a prayer mat lit by a single moonbeam through an arched window, stars overhead.

Arabic: سُبْحَانَ الْمَلِكِ الْقُدُّوسِ
Translation: Glory be to the Sovereign, the Most Holy
Benefit Text: Recited 3 times in Witr prayer, raising the voice on the third.
Illustration: Standing figure in prayer with hands raised, a single luminous drop of mercy reflecting moonlight beside them.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ اهْدِنِي فِيمَنْ هَدَيْتَ وَعَافِنِي فِيمَنْ عَافَيْتَ وَتَوَلَّنِي فِيمَنْ تَوَلَّيْتَ
Translation: O Allah, guide me among those You have guided, grant me well-being among those You have granted well-being, and befriend me among those You have befriended
Benefit Text: Dua al-Qunut recited in Witr prayer.
Illustration: Standing figure in prayer with hands raised, a single luminous drop of mercy reflecting moonlight beside them.

Arabic: اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ سُبْحَانَ الَّذِي سَخَّرَ لَنَا هَذَا وَمَا كُنَّا لَهُ مُقْرِنِينَ وَإِنَّا إِلَى رَبِّنَا لَمُنقَلِبُونَ
Translation: Allah is the Greatest (3x). Glory be to Him who has made this subservient to us, and we were not capable of that, and indeed to our Lord we will return
Benefit Text: Said when mounting a vehicle or beginning a journey.
Illustration: Open desert road at sunrise, a traveller silhouette beside a vehicle, distant mountains and a rising sun on the horizon.

Arabic: آيِبُونَ تَائِبُونَ عَابِدُونَ لِرَبِّنَا حَامِدُونَ
Translation: Returning, repenting, worshipping, and praising our Lord
Benefit Text: Said after completing a journey — the Prophet would say this upon returning.
Illustration: Open desert road at sunrise, a traveller silhouette beside a vehicle, distant mountains and a rising sun on the horizon.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ صَلِّ عَلَى مُحَمَّدٍ وَعَلَى آلِ مُحَمَّدٍ كَمَا صَلَّيْتَ عَلَى إِبْرَاهِيمَ وَعَلَى آلِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ إِنَّكَ حَمِيدٌ مَجِيدٌ
Translation: O Allah, send prayers upon Muhammad and the family of Muhammad, as You sent prayers upon Ibrahim and the family of Ibrahim. Indeed You are the Most Praiseworthy, Most Glorious
Benefit Text: Whoever sends one blessing upon the Prophet, Allah sends ten upon him.
Illustration: Pink and white rose petals drifting upward toward elegant calligraphy of the Prophet’s name, soft gold halo around it.

Arabic: صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ
Translation: May Allah's peace and blessings be upon him
Benefit Text: Allah sends ten blessings upon anyone who sends one upon the Prophet.
Illustration: Pink and white rose petals drifting upward toward elegant calligraphy of the Prophet’s name, soft gold halo around it.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ صَلِّ عَلَى مُحَمَّدٍ
Translation: O Allah, send prayers upon Muhammad — increase abundantly on Fridays
Benefit Text: The Prophet commanded us to increase prayers upon him on Friday — they are presented to him on that day.
Illustration: Pink and white rose petals drifting upward toward elegant calligraphy of the Prophet’s name, soft gold halo around it.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ أَنْتَ حَسَّنْتَ خَلْقِي فَحَسِّنْ خُلُقِي
Translation: O Allah, just as You made my physical form beautiful, make my character beautiful too
Benefit Text: Dua when looking in the mirror.
Illustration: A person standing before a softly glowing mirror, reflection gently radiant, hands lifted in quiet supplication.

Arabic: أَعُوذُ بِاللَّهِ مِنَ الشَّيْطَانِ الرَّجِيمِ
Translation: I seek refuge in Allah from the accursed Shaytan
Benefit Text: The Prophet said: If you are angry, seek refuge in Allah from Shaytan — anger will leave.
Illustration: A seated figure with eyes closed, a dark cloud of anger lifting away from the head into the wind.

Arabic: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ
Translation: All praise is for Allah — the one who hears responds: May Allah have mercy on you
Benefit Text: When you sneeze, say Alhamdulillah. Those who hear say Yarhamukallah.
Illustration: A person covering the nose with cloth, gentle dust particles drifting into a single warm sunbeam.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ خَيْرَ الْمَوْلِجِ وَخَيْرَ الْمَخْرَجِ
Translation: O Allah, I ask You for the best entrance and the best exit
Benefit Text: Du'a when entering the house.
Illustration: Warm doorway of a home at dusk, a figure stepping over the threshold with hand softly raised in remembrance.

Arabic: رَبَّنَا آتِنَا فِي الدُّنْيَا حَسَنَةً وَفِي الْآخِرَةِ حَسَنَةً وَقِنَا عَذَابَ النَّارِ
Translation: Our Lord, give us good in this world and good in the Hereafter and protect us from the punishment of the Fire
Benefit Text: The most comprehensive du'a — the Prophet used to say it often.
Illustration: Two open hands cupping a small green Earth on one side and a luminous miniature Jannah orb on the other.

Arabic: لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا أَنتَ سُبْحَانَكَ إِنِّي كُنتُ مِنَ الظَّالِمِينَ
Translation: There is no deity except You; exalted are You. Indeed, I have been of the wrongdoers
Benefit Text: The du'a of Prophet Yunus. No Muslim calls upon Allah with it except that Allah responds.
Illustration: A small lone figure within softly-lit deep blue water, a single ray of mercy piercing the dark from above.

Arabic: حَسْبِيَ اللَّهُ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا هُوَ عَلَيْهِ تَوَكَّلْتُ وَهُوَ رَبُّ الْعَرْشِ الْعَظِيمِ
Translation: Sufficient for me is Allah; there is no deity except Him. On Him I have relied, and He is the Lord of the Great Throne
Benefit Text: Whoever says this 7 times morning and evening, Allah suffices him.
Illustration: A pair of open hands raised in quiet supplication beneath a soft halo of light, prayer beads resting nearby.

Arabic: رَبَّنَا ظَلَمْنَا أَنفُسَنَا وَإِن لَّمْ تَغْفِرْ لَنَا وَتَرْحَمْنَا لَنَكُونَنَّ مِنَ الْخَاسِرِينَ
Translation: Our Lord, we have wronged ourselves; and if You do not forgive us and have mercy on us, we will surely be among the losers
Benefit Text: The du'a of Prophet Adam and Hawwa — said when seeking forgiveness.
Illustration: An open Quran on a wooden rehl with a soft gold ray rising from the page, hands gently cupped to receive it.

Arabic: أَسْتَغْفِرُ اللَّهَ
Translation: I seek forgiveness from Allah
Benefit Text: The Prophet used to seek forgiveness 70+ times daily. Whoever does Istighfar, Allah relieves his distress and provides from where he cannot imagine.
Illustration: Hands cupped in repentance, soft drops of forgiving light falling from above onto the open palms.

Arabic: أَسْتَغْفِرُ اللَّهَ وَأَتُوبُ إِلَيْهِ
Translation: I seek Allah's forgiveness and repent to Him
Benefit Text: The Prophet said: I seek forgiveness 70 times daily.
Illustration: Hands cupped in repentance, soft drops of forgiving light falling from above onto the open palms.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ أَنْتَ رَبِّي لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ خَلَقْتَنِي وَأَنَا عَبْدُكَ وَأَنَا عَلَى عَهْدِكَ وَوَعْدِكَ مَا اسْتَطَعْتُ أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا صَنَعْتُ أَبُوءُ لَكَ بِنِعْمَتِكَ عَلَيَّ وَأَبُوءُ بِذَنْبِي فَاغْفِرْ لِي فَإِنَّهُ لَا يَغْفِرُ الذُّنُوبَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ
Translation: O Allah, You are my Lord. None has the right to be worshipped but You. You created me and I am Your servant. I abide by Your covenant and Your promise as best I can. I seek refuge in You from the evil of what I have done. I acknowledge before You Your blessings upon me and I acknowledge my sins. So forgive me, for none forgives sins but You.
Benefit Text: The master of all prayers for forgiveness. Whoever says it with firm belief in the morning and dies that day will be among the people of Paradise.
Illustration: Two tall gates of Paradise swinging slowly open, warm golden light pouring through onto a kneeling figure with head bowed.

Arabic: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي أَحْيَانَا بَعْدَ مَا أَمَاتَنَا وَإِلَيْهِ النُّشُورُ
Translation: All praise is for Allah who gave us life after having taken it from us, and to Him is the resurrection
Benefit Text: The first words of the Prophet upon waking from sleep.
Illustration: A sleeping figure beneath a soft blanket, a crescent moon and stars above, a gentle guardian-light hovering nearby.

Arabic: لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لَا شَرِيكَ لَهُ
Translation: There is no god but Allah alone, with no partner
Benefit Text: Whoever says this 10 times upon waking has 10 good deeds written and 10 sins erased.
Illustration: A figure sitting upright on the edge of a bed at dawn, soft golden sunrise spilling through an open window onto the floor.

Arabic: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي كَسَانِي هَذَا وَرَزَقَنِيهِ مِنْ غَيْرِ حَوْلٍ مِنِّي وَلَا قُوَّةٍ
Translation: All praise is for Allah who has clothed me with this and provided it for me without any power from myself
Benefit Text: Whoever says this when putting on a garment has his past sins forgiven.
Illustration: A neatly folded white garment resting on a warm wooden surface, soft light rising from its folds.

Arabic: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ
Translation: In the name of Allah
Benefit Text: There is no wudu for the one who does not begin with the name of Allah.
Illustration: Clear water flowing gently over open hands above a small brass basin, soft droplets catching the light.

Arabic: أَشْهَدُ أَنْ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَأَشْهَدُ أَنَّ مُحَمَّدًا عَبْدُهُ وَرَسُولُهُ
Translation: I testify there is no god but Allah and that Muhammad is His servant and messenger
Benefit Text: Whoever says this after wudu — the eight gates of Paradise open for him.
Illustration: Clear water flowing gently over open hands above a small brass basin, soft droplets catching the light.

Arabic: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ
Translation: In the name of Allah
Benefit Text: When you eat, say Bismillah. If you forget at the start, say Bismillah in its beginning and end.
Illustration: A simple wooden table with bread, dates, and a cup of water, hands gently raised in gratitude above the meal.

Arabic: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي أَطْعَمَنَا وَسَقَانَا وَجَعَلَنَا مُسْلِمِينَ
Translation: All praise is for Allah who fed us, gave us drink, and made us Muslims
Benefit Text: Dua after eating.
Illustration: A simple wooden table with bread, dates, and a cup of water, hands gently raised in gratitude above the meal.

Arabic: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ وَلَجْنَا وَبِسْمِ اللَّهِ خَرَجْنَا وَعَلَى اللَّهِ رَبِّنَا تَوَكَّلْنَا
Translation: In the name of Allah we enter, in the name of Allah we leave, and upon Allah our Lord we rely
Benefit Text: Say this when entering the home — Shaytan is turned away and cannot spend the night.
Illustration: Warm doorway of a home at dusk, a figure stepping over the threshold with hand softly raised in remembrance.

Arabic: أَعُوذُ بِكَلِمَاتِ اللَّهِ التَّامَّاتِ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا خَلَقَ
Translation: I seek refuge in the perfect words of Allah from the evil of what He has created
Benefit Text: Whoever says this when arriving at a place — nothing will harm him until he leaves.
Illustration: Warm doorway of a home at dusk, a figure stepping over the threshold with hand softly raised in remembrance.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْتَخِيرُكَ بِعِلْمِكَ وَأَسْتَقْدِرُكَ بِقُدْرَتِكَ وَأَسْأَلُكَ مِنْ فَضْلِكَ الْعَظِيمِ فَإِنَّكَ تَقْدِرُ وَلَا أَقْدِرُ وَتَعْلَمُ وَلَا أَعْلَمُ وَأَنتَ عَلَّامُ الْغُيُوبِ
Translation: O Allah, I seek Your guidance by Your knowledge, I seek ability by Your power, and I ask You of Your great favour — for You have power and I have none, You know and I do not know, and You are the Knower of the unseen
Benefit Text: Prayed after 2 rak'ahs when making a decision. Allah then facilitates what is best.
Illustration: A figure standing at a quiet crossroads under a starry sky, a single bright guiding star directly above the path ahead.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ افْتَحْ لِي أَبْوَابَ رَحْمَتِكَ
Translation: O Allah, open for me the gates of Your mercy
Benefit Text: Said when entering the mosque.
Illustration: A warm wooden mosque doorway opening onto a soft inner light, a figure stepping over the threshold with hands raised.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ مِنْ فَضْلِكَ
Translation: O Allah, I ask You of Your bounty
Benefit Text: Said when leaving the mosque.
Illustration: A warm wooden mosque doorway opening onto a soft inner light, a figure stepping over the threshold with hands raised.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ رَبَّ هَذِهِ الدَّعْوَةِ التَّامَّةِ وَالصَّلَاةِ الْقَائِمَةِ آتِ مُحَمَّدًا الْوَسِيلَةَ وَالْفَضِيلَةَ
Translation: O Allah, Lord of this perfect call and the established prayer, grant Muhammad the highest rank and honour
Benefit Text: Whoever says this after the adhan will have the Prophet's intercession on Judgment Day.
Illustration: A minaret silhouette against a pale dawn sky, soft circular waves of sound radiating outward over a sleeping city.

Arabic: حَسْبُنَا اللَّهُ وَنِعْمَ الْوَكِيلُ
Translation: Allah is sufficient for us and He is the best Disposer of affairs
Benefit Text: Said by Ibrahim when thrown in the fire. Said by the Prophet when told people had gathered against him.
Illustration: A small figure standing calmly inside soft flames that have turned to gentle gardens of light around them.

Arabic: إِنَّا لِلَّهِ وَإِنَّا إِلَيْهِ رَاجِعُونَ
Translation: Indeed we belong to Allah, and indeed to Him we will return
Benefit Text: Said in times of calamity — Allah's blessings and mercy descend upon the one who says it.
Illustration: A small figure looking upward at a soft beam of mercy from above, gentle blossoms drifting down around them.

Arabic: لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا أَنتَ سُبْحَانَكَ إِنِّي كُنتُ مِنَ الظَّالِمِينَ
Translation: There is no god but You, glory be to You, I have indeed been among the wrongdoers
Benefit Text: No Muslim calls upon Allah with it except that Allah responds.
Illustration: A small lone figure within softly-lit deep blue water, a single ray of mercy piercing the dark from above.

Arabic: أَعُوذُ بِاللَّهِ السَّمِيعِ الْعَلِيمِ مِنَ الشَّيْطَانِ الرَّجِيمِ
Translation: I seek refuge in Allah, the All-Hearing, All-Knowing, from the accursed Shaytan
Benefit Text: Said when feeling waswas or doubts in the heart.
Illustration: A small glowing heart shielded by a translucent dome of light, dark whispering wisps held back at its surface.

Arabic: قُلْ أَعُوذُ بِرَبِّ الْفَلَقِ
Translation: Say: I seek refuge in the Lord of the daybreak (recite Al-Falaq and An-Nas 3 times)
Benefit Text: Recite Al-Falaq and An-Nas 3 times morning and evening — they suffice against all harm.
Illustration: Open Quran on Surah Al-Falaq, dawn breaking on the horizon, a dark knot of evil dissolving into the morning light.

Arabic: تَوَكَّلْتُ عَلَى اللَّهِ وَلَا حَوْلَ وَلَا قُوَّةَ إِلَّا بِاللَّهِ
Translation: I put my trust in Allah, and there is no might or power except with Allah
Benefit Text: Whoever says this when leaving his house: it is said to him — you are guided, defended and protected.
Illustration: A small glowing heart shielded by a translucent dome of light, dark whispering wisps held back at its surface.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ خَيْرَ هَذِهِ السُّوقِ وَخَيْرَ مَا فِيهَا وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّهَا وَشَرِّ مَا فِيهَا
Translation: O Allah, I ask You for the good of this marketplace and the good that is in it, and I seek refuge in You from its evil and the evil that is in it
Benefit Text: Whoever says this entering the marketplace — 1 million good deeds written, 1 million sins erased.
Illustration: A soft silhouette stepping into a covered bazaar at dawn, lanterns glowing above stalls, hands raised in quiet remembrance.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ أَلِّفْ بَيْنَ قُلُوبِنَا وَأَصْلِحْ ذَاتَ بَيْنِنَا
Translation: O Allah, unite our hearts and set right our affairs between us
Benefit Text: Du'a for harmony in family and marriage.
Illustration: Two silhouettes side by side beneath a soft canopy of light, small star-blossoms drifting gently down between them.

Arabic: بَارَكَ اللَّهُ لَكَ وَبَارَكَ عَلَيْكَ وَجَمَعَ بَيْنَكُمَا فِي خَيْرٍ
Translation: May Allah bless you and shower blessings upon you and join you together in goodness
Benefit Text: The Sunnah congratulation for a newlywed couple.
Illustration: Pink and white rose petals drifting upward toward elegant calligraphy of the Prophet’s name, soft gold halo around it.

Arabic: السَّلَامُ عَلَيْكُمْ وَرَحْمَةُ اللَّهِ وَبَرَكَاتُهُ
Translation: Peace be upon you and the mercy and blessings of Allah
Benefit Text: Spreading salaam is a means of entering Paradise. The full greeting earns 30 hasanat.
Illustration: Two figures meeting on a soft path, hands placed gently over their hearts, a warm halo of greeting between them.

Arabic: سُبْحَانَكَ اللَّهُمَّ وَبِحَمْدِكَ أَشْهَدُ أَنْ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا أَنتَ أَسْتَغْفِرُكَ وَأَتُوبُ إِلَيْكَ
Translation: Glory be to You O Allah and Your praise. I testify there is no god but You. I seek Your forgiveness and repent to You
Benefit Text: Kaffarat al-majlis — expiation for any gathering. Said at the end of every sitting.
Illustration: Hands cupped in repentance, soft drops of forgiving light falling from above onto the open palms.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ صَيِّبًا نَافِعًا
Translation: O Allah, make it beneficial rain
Benefit Text: The Prophet would expose himself to rain saying this.
Illustration: Soft falling rain over green earth at dawn, two open hands raised to receive the drops, faint sunlight breaking through the clouds.

Arabic: سُبْحَانَ الَّذِي يُسَبِّحُ الرَّعْدُ بِحَمْدِهِ
Translation: Glory be to Him whom the thunder glorifies with His praise
Benefit Text: Said when thunder is heard.
Illustration: A wide night sky with a single quiet flash of light over distant mountains, soft cloud forms hushed in glorification.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ اغْفِرْ لَهُ وَارْحَمْهُ وَعَافِهِ وَاعْفُ عَنْهُ
Translation: O Allah, forgive him, have mercy on him, grant him well-being, and pardon him
Benefit Text: Janazah prayer dua — pray fervently for the deceased.
Illustration: A simple white-shrouded form at peace under a soft starlit sky, a gentle column of mercy-light descending from above.

Arabic: إِنَّا لِلَّهِ وَإِنَّا إِلَيْهِ رَاجِعُونَ
Translation: Indeed we belong to Allah, and indeed to Him we will return
Benefit Text: Said upon the death of someone — reminds us all belongs to Allah.
Illustration: A small figure looking upward at a soft beam of mercy from above, gentle blossoms drifting down around them.

Arabic: سُبْحَانَكَ اللَّهُمَّ وَبِحَمْدِكَ تَبَارَكَ اسْمُكَ وَتَعَالَى جَدُّكَ وَلَا إِلَهَ غَيْرُكَ
Translation: Glory be to You O Allah and Your praise, blessed is Your name, exalted is Your majesty, and there is no god but You
Benefit Text: Opening supplication of gatherings.
Illustration: A small circle of seated silhouettes beneath soft hanging lanterns, a gentle gold light closing softly over the group.

Arabic: سُبْحَانَكَ اللَّهُمَّ وَبِحَمْدِكَ أَشْهَدُ أَنْ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا أَنتَ أَسْتَغْفِرُكَ وَأَتُوبُ إِلَيْكَ
Translation: Glory be to You O Allah and Your praise. I testify there is no god but You. I seek Your forgiveness and repent to You
Benefit Text: Expiation of the gathering — seals the session and forgives what was said.
Illustration: Hands cupped in repentance, soft drops of forgiving light falling from above onto the open palms.

Arabic: لَبَّيْكَ اللَّهُمَّ لَبَّيْكَ لَبَّيْكَ لَا شَرِيكَ لَكَ لَبَّيْكَ إِنَّ الْحَمْدَ وَالنِّعْمَةَ لَكَ وَالْمُلْكَ لَا شَرِيكَ لَكَ
Translation: Here I am O Allah, here I am. Here I am, You have no partner. Verily all praise, grace and sovereignty belong to You. You have no partner
Benefit Text: The Talbiyah of Hajj and Umrah — said continuously from Ihram until Tawaf. Every stone and tree joins in.
Illustration: Distant view of the Kaaba in soft golden light, a wide circle of pilgrims raising their voices, hands lifted in declaration.

Arabic: سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ وَالْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ وَلَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَاللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ
Translation: Glory be to Allah, all praise is for Allah, there is no god but Allah, and Allah is the Greatest
Benefit Text: Said during tawaf around the Ka'bah — the four phrases most beloved to Allah.
Illustration: Concentric rings of pilgrims circling the Kaaba in soft golden light, a single figure with hand raised in greeting.

Arabic: أَعُوذُ بِكَلِمَاتِ اللَّهِ التَّامَّةِ مِنْ غَضَبِهِ وَعِقَابِهِ وَشَرِّ عِبَادِهِ وَمِنْ هَمَزَاتِ الشَّيَاطِينِ
Translation: I seek refuge in the perfect words of Allah from His anger, punishment, the evil of His servants, and from the whispers of devils
Benefit Text: Said upon waking from a bad dream — then spit to the left 3 times and the dream will not harm you.
Illustration: A figure sitting upright on the edge of a bed at dawn, soft golden sunrise spilling through an open window onto the floor.

Arabic: أَعُوذُ بِاللَّهِ مِنَ الشَّيْطَانِ الرَّجِيمِ
Translation: I seek refuge in Allah from the accursed Shaytan
Benefit Text: When you see a bad dream: spit to your left 3 times, say this 3 times, and change your side.
Illustration: A pair of open hands raised in quiet supplication beneath a soft halo of light, prayer beads resting nearby.

Arabic: سُبْحَانَ اللهِ اَلْحَمْدُ لِلهِ اَللهُ اَكْبَرُ
Translation: Glorious is Allah (33). Praises are due to Allah (33). Allah is the greatest (34).
Benefit Text: Narrated `Ali bin Abi Talib (RA): Fatima came to the Prophet (ﷺ) asking for a servant. He said, "May I inform you of something better than that? When you go to bed, recite Subhan Allah thirty-three times, Al-hamdu li-lah thirty-three times, and Allahu Akbar thirty-four times."
Illustration: A sleeping figure beneath a soft blanket, a crescent moon and stars above, a gentle guardian-light hovering nearby.

Arabic: بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمٰنِ الرَّحِيْمِ. قُلْ هُوَ اللهُ اَحَدٌ. اَللهُ الصَّمَدُ. لَمْ يَلِدْ وَلَمْ يُوْلَدْ. وَلَمْ يَكُنْ لَّهُۥ كُفُوًا اَحَدٌ.
Translation: In the name of Allah, the Entirely Merciful, the Especially Merciful. Say, "He is Allah, [who is] One. Allah, the Eternal Refuge. He neither begets nor is born. Nor is there to Him any equivalent."
Benefit Text: Narrated `Aisha (RA): Whenever the Prophet (ﷺ) went to bed every night, he used to cup his hands together and blow over it after reciting Surat Al-Ikhlas, Surat Al-Falaq and Surat An-Nas, and then rub his hands over whatever parts of his body he was able to rub, starting with his head, face and front of his body. He used to do that three times.
Illustration: Open Quran turned to Surah Al-Ikhlas, calligraphy glowing, a single soft ray of light rising from the page.

Arabic: بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمٰنِ الرَّحِيْمِ. قُلْ اَعُوْذُ بِرَبِّ الْفَلَقِ. مِنْ شَرِّ مَا خَلَقَ. وَمِنْ شَرِّ غَاسِقٍ اِذَا وَقَبَ. وَمِنْ شَرِّ النَّفّٰثٰتِ فِى الْعُقَدِ. وَمِنْ شَرِّ حَاسِدٍ اِذَا حَسَدَ.
Translation: In the name of Allah, the Entirely Merciful, the Especially Merciful. Say, "I seek refuge in the Lord of daybreak. From the evil of that which He created. And from the evil of darkness when it settles. And from the evil of the blowers in knots. And from the evil of an envier when he envies."
Benefit Text: Narrated `Aisha (RA): Whenever the Prophet (ﷺ) went to bed every night, he used to cup his hands together and blow over it after reciting Surat Al-Ikhlas, Surat Al-Falaq and Surat An-Nas, and then rub his hands over whatever parts of his body he was able to rub, starting with his head, face and front of his body. He used to do that three times.
Illustration: Open Quran turned to Surah Al-Ikhlas, calligraphy glowing, a single soft ray of light rising from the page.

Arabic: بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمٰنِ الرَّحِيْمِ. قُلْ اَعُوْذُ بِرَبِّ النَّاسِ. مَلِكِ النَّاسِ. اِلٰهِ النَّاسِ. مِنْ شَرِّ الْوَسْوَاسِ الْخَنَّاسِ. الَّذِىْ يُوَسْوِسُ فِىْ صُدُوْرِ النَّاسِ. مِنَ الْجِنَّةِ وَالنَّاسِ.
Translation: In the name of Allah, the Entirely Merciful, the Especially Merciful. Say, "I seek refuge in the Lord of mankind. The Sovereign of mankind. The God of mankind. From the evil of the retreating whisperer. Who whispers [evil] into the breasts of mankind. From among the jinn and mankind."
Benefit Text: Narrated `Aisha (RA): Whenever the Prophet (ﷺ) went to bed every night, he used to cup his hands together and blow over it after reciting Surat Al-Ikhlas, Surat Al-Falaq and Surat An-Nas, and then rub his hands over whatever parts of his body he was able to rub, starting with his head, face and front of his body. He used to do that three times.
Illustration: Open Quran turned to Surah Al-Ikhlas, calligraphy glowing, a single soft ray of light rising from the page.

Arabic: بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمٰنِ الرَّحِيْمِ. اَللّٰهُ لَآ اِلٰهَ اِلَّا هُوَ الْحَىُّ الْقَيُّوْمُ، لَا تَأْخُذُهُۥ سِنَةٌ وَّلَا نَوْمٌ، لَهُ مَا فِى السَّمٰوٰتِ وَمَا فِى الْاَرْضِ، مَنْ ذَا الَّذِىْ يَشْفَعُ عِنْدَهُۥ اِلَّا بِاِذْنِهِۦ، يَعْلَمُ مَا بَيْنَ اَيْدِيْهِمْ وَمَا خَلْفَهُمْ، وَلَا يُحِيْطُوْنَ بِشَىْءٍ مِّنْ عِلْمِهِۦۤ اِلَّا بِمَا شَآءَ، وَسِعَ كُرْسِيُّهُ السَّمٰوٰتِ وَالْاَرْضَ، وَلَا يَئُوْدُهُۥ حِفْظُهُمَا، وَهُوَ الْعَلِيُّ الْعَظِيْمُ.
Translation: In the name of Allah, the Entirely Merciful, the Especially Merciful. Allah – there is no deity except Him, the Ever-Living, the Sustainer of [all] existence. Neither drowsiness overtakes Him nor sleep. To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. Who is it that can intercede with Him except by His permission? He knows what is [presently] before them and what will be after them, and they encompass not a thing of His knowledge except for what He wills. His Kursi extends over the heavens and the earth, and their preservation tires Him not. And He is the Most High, the Most Great.
Benefit Text: Narrated by Abu Huraira (RA): Allah's Apostle (ﷺ) ordered me to guard the Zakat revenue of Ramadan. Then somebody came to me and started stealing of the foodstuff. I caught him and said: "I will take you to Allah's Messenger (ﷺ)." Then Abu Huraira described the whole narration and said, that person said to me: "Please don't take me to Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) and I will tell you a few words by which Allah will benefit you." He said: "When you go to your bed, recite Ayatul Kursi (2:255) for then there will be a guard from Allah who will protect you all night long, and Shaytan will not be able to come near you till dawn." When the Prophet (ﷺ) heard the story he said to me: "He who came to you at night, told you the truth although he is a liar and it was Shaytan."
Illustration: Glowing celestial throne extending across the heavens and earth, with a soft golden dome of protection forming over a kneeling worshipper.

Arabic: اٰمَنَ الرَّسُوْلُ بِمَآ اُنْزِلَ اِلَيْهِ مِنْ رَّبِّهٖ وَالْمُؤْمِنُوْنَ، كُلٌّ اٰمَنَ بِاللّٰهِ وَمَلٰٓئِكَتِهٖ وَكُتُبِهٖ وَرُسُلِهٖ، لَا نُفَرِّقُ بَيْنَ اَحَدٍ مِّنْ رُّسُلِهٖ، وَقَالُوْا سَمِعْنَا وَاَطَعْنَا، غُفْرَانَكَ رَبَّنَا وَاِلَيْكَ الْمَصِيْرُ.
Translation: The Messenger has believed in what was revealed to him from his Lord, and [so have] the believers. All of them have believed in Allah and His angels and His books and His messengers, [saying], We make no distinction between any of His messengers. And they say, We hear and we obey. [We seek] Your forgiveness, our Lord, and to You is the [final] destination.
Benefit Text: Narrated Abu Mas`ud al-Ansari (RA): The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "If one recites the last two verses of Surat al-Baqarah at night, it is sufficient for him (for that night)."
Illustration: Cupped hands holding a soft, weighted light of guidance, an unburdened figure standing upright beside.

Arabic: لَا يُكَلِّفُ اللّٰهُ نَفْسًا اِلَّا وُسْعَهَا، لَهَا مَا كَسَبَتْ وَعَلَيْهَا مَا اكْتَسَبَتْ، رَبَّنَا لَا تُؤَاخِذْنَآ اِنْ نَّسِيْنَآ اَوْ اَخْطَأْنَا، رَبَّنَا وَلَا تَحْمِلْ عَلَيْنَآ اِصْرًا كَمَا حَمَلْتَهٗ عَلَى الَّذِيْنَ مِنْ قَبْلِنَا، رَبَّنَا وَلَا تُحَمِّلْنَا مَا لَا طَاقَةَ لَنَا بِهٖ، وَاعْفُ عَنَّا، وَاغْفِرْ لَنَا، وَارْحَمْنَا، اَنْتَ مَوْلٰىنَا، فَانْصُرْنَا عَلَى الْقَوْمِ الْكٰفِرِيْنَ.
Translation: Allah does not charge a soul except [with that within] its capacity. It will have [the consequence of] what [good] it has gained, and it will bear [the consequence of] what [evil] it has earned. Our Lord, do not impose blame upon us if we have forgotten or erred. Our Lord, and lay not upon us a burden like that which You laid upon those before us. Our Lord, and burden us not with that which we have no ability to bear. And pardon us; and forgive us; and have mercy upon us. You are our protector, so give us victory over the disbelieving people.
Benefit Text: Narrated Abu Mas`ud al-Ansari (RA): The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "If one recites the last two verses of Surat al-Baqarah at night, it is sufficient for him (for that night)."
