Illustration: A sleeping figure beneath a soft blanket, a crescent moon and stars above, a gentle guardian-light hovering nearby.

Arabic: بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمٰنِ الرَّحِيْمِ. قُلْ يٰۤاَيُّهَا الْكٰفِرُوْنَ. لَآ اَعْبُدُ مَا تَعْبُدُوْنَ. وَلَآ اَنْتُمْ عٰبِدُوْنَ مَآ اَعْبُدُ. وَلَآ اَنَا عَابِدٌ مَّا عَبَدْتُّمْ. وَلَآ اَنْتُمْ عٰبِدُوْنَ مَآ اَعْبُدُ. لَكُمْ دِيْنُكُمْ وَلِيَ دِيْنِ.
Translation: In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful. Say, "O disbelievers, I do not worship what you worship. Nor are you worshippers of what I worship. Nor will I be a worshipper of what you worship. Nor will you be worshippers of what I worship. For you is your religion, and for me is my religion."
Benefit Text: Farwah b. Nawfal (RA) quoted his father as saying that the Prophet (ﷺ) said to Nawfal (his father): Recite (Al-Kafirun 109) 'Say, O you disbelievers!' and then go to sleep at its end, for it is a declaration of freedom from polytheism.
Illustration: Open Quran on Surah Al-Kafirun, a clear straight path of light forking cleanly away from a crumbling pile of idols.

Arabic: اَللّٰهُمَّ بِاسْمِكَ اَمُوْتُ وَاَحْيَا
Translation: O Allah in Your name, I die and I live.
Benefit Text: Narrated Abu Dhar (RA): Whenever the Prophet (ﷺ) lay on his bed, he used to say: "Allahumma bismika amutu wa ahya," and when he woke up he would say: "Al-hamdu lil-lahilladhi ahyana ba'da ma an atana, wa ilaihi an-nushur."
Illustration: Sleeping figure under a crescent moon on one side, the same figure rising in soft dawn light on the other side.

Arabic: اَللّٰهُمَّ اَسْلَمْتُ وَجْهِيْ اِلَيْكَ، وَفَوَّضْتُ اَمْرِىْ اِلَيْكَ، وَاَلْجَأْتُ ظَهْرِيْ اِلَيْكَ، رَغْبَةً وَّرَهْبَةً اِلَيْكَ، لَا مَلْجَأَ وَلَا مَنْجَا مِنْكَ اِلَّآ اِلَيْكَ، اٰمَنْتُ بِكِتَابِكَ الَّذِىْۤ اَنْزَلْتَ، وَبِنَبِيِّكَ الَّذِىْۤ اَرْسَلْتَ.
Translation: O Allah! I have submitted myself to You, I have turned my face to You, committed my affairs to You, and depend on You for protection out of desire for You and out of fear of You (expecting Your reward and fearing Your punishment). There is no refuge and no place of safety from You but with You. I believed in the Book You have revealed, and in the Prophet You have sent (i.e., Muhammad ﷺ).
Benefit Text: Narrated Al-Bara bin `Azib (RA): Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said to me, When you want to go to bed, perform ablution as you do for prayer, then lie down on your right side and say: 'Allahumma aslamtu wajhi ilaika, wa fawwadtu 'amri ilaika, wa alja'tu dhahri ilaika, raghbatan wa rahbatan ilaika, la malja'a wa la manja minka illa ilaika. Aamantu bikitabik al-ladhi anzalta wa binabiyyik al-ladhi arsalta'. If you should die then (after reciting this) you will die on the religion of Islam (i.e., as a Muslim); so let these words be the last you say (before going to bed) While I was memorizing it, I said, Wa birasulika al-ladhi arsalta (in Your Apostle whom You have sent).' The Prophet (ﷺ) said, No, but say: Wa binabiyyika al-ladhi arsalta (in Your Prophet whom You have sent).
Illustration: A sleeping figure beneath a soft blanket, a crescent moon and stars above, a gentle guardian-light hovering nearby.

Arabic: بِاسْمِكَ رَبِّيْ وَضَعْتُ جَنْبِيْ، وَبِكَ اَرْفَعُهٗ، اِنْ اَمْسَكْتَ نَفْسِىْ فَارْحَمْهَا، وَاِنْ اَرْسَلْتَهَا فَاحْفَظْهَا، بِمَا تَحْفَظُ بِهٖ عِبَادَكَ الصَّالِحِيْنَ
Translation: With Your Name, my Lord, I place my side upon the bed, and with Your Grace I will raise it up. If You withhold my soul (cause me to die), have mercy on it but if You let it go (let me live), guard it against which You guarded Your pious slaves.
Benefit Text: Narrated Abu Huraira (RA): The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "When anyone of you go to bed, he should shake out his bed with the inside of his waist sheet, for he does not know what has come on to it after him, and then he should say: 'Bismika Rabbi Wada`tu Janbi wa bika arfa'uhu, In amsakta nafsi farhamha wa in arsaltaha fahfazha bima tahfazu bihi ibadakas-salihin."
Illustration: A hand gently smoothing the surface of a moonlit bed, a soft white blanket folded back to receive a sleeper.

Arabic: اَللّٰهُمَّ قِنِيْ عَذَابَكَ يَوْمَ تَبْعَثُ عِبَادَكَ
Translation: O Allah! Guard me against Your punishment on the Day when You will resurrect Your slaves.
Benefit Text: Narrated Hafsah, Ummul Mu'minin (RA): When the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) wanted to go to sleep, he put his right hand under his cheek and would then say three times: O Allah! Guard me against Your punishment on the Day when You will resurrect Your slaves.
Illustration: A sleeping figure beneath a soft blanket, a crescent moon and stars above, a gentle guardian-light hovering nearby.

Arabic: اَلْحَمْدُ لِلّٰهِ الَّذِىْۤ اَطْعَمَنَا وَسَقَانَا، وَكَفَانَا وَاٰوَانَا، فَكَمْ مِّمَّنْ لَّا كَافِىَ لَهٗ وَلَا مُؤْوِىَ
Translation: All praise is for Allah, Who fed us and gave us drink, and Who is sufficient for us and has sheltered us, for how many have none to suffice them or shelter them.
Benefit Text: Anas (RA) reported Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) as saying: When you go to bed, say: "Praise is due to Allah Who fed us, provided us drink, sufficed us and provided us with shelter, for many a people there is none to suffice and none to provide shelter."
Illustration: A sleeping figure beneath a soft blanket, a crescent moon and stars above, a gentle guardian-light hovering nearby.

Arabic: اَللّٰهُمَّ اِنَّكَ خَلَقْتَ نَفْسِيْ، وَاَنْتَ تَوَفَّاهَا، لَكَ مَمَاتُهَا وَمَحْيَاهَا، اِنْ اَحْيَيْتَهَا فَاحْفَظْهَا، وَاِنْ اَمَتَّهَا فَاغْفِرْ لَهَا، اَللّٰهُمَّ اِنِّيْۤ اَسْاَلُكَ الْعَافِيَةَ
Translation: O Allah, You have created my soul and it is for You to take it in death. Its death and its life are in Your hand. If You cause it to live then protect it and if You cause it to die then forgive it. O Allah, I ask You to keep me safe and sound.
Benefit Text: Abdullah b. 'Umar (RA) commanded a person that as he went to bed, he should say: "O Allah, Thou created my being and it is for Thee to take it to its ultimate goal. And its death and life is due to Thee, and if Thou givest it life, safeguard it; and if Thou bringst death, grant it pardon. O Allah, I beg of Thee safety." A person said to him: Did you hear it from Umar? Thereupon he said: (I have heard from one) who is better than Umar, viz. from Allah's Messenger (ﷺ). Ibn Nafi reported this on the authority of Abdullah b. Harith but he did not make mention of this that he heard it himself.
Illustration: A sleeping figure beneath a soft blanket, a crescent moon and stars above, a gentle guardian-light hovering nearby.

Arabic: اَللّٰهُمَّ رَبَّ السَّمٰوٰتِ وَرَبَّ الْاَرْضِ وَرَبَّ الْعَرْشِ الْعَظِيْمِ، رَبَّنَا وَرَبَّ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ، فَالِقَ الْحَبِّ وَالنَّوٰى وَمُنْزِلَ التَّوْرَاةِ وَالْاِنْجِيْلِ وَالْفُرْقَانِ، اَعُوْذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ اَنْتَ اٰخِذٌ بِنَاصِيَتِهٖ، اَللّٰهُمَّ اَنْتَ الْاَوَّلُ فَلَيْسَ قَبْلَكَ شَىْءٌ، وَاَنْتَ الْاٰخِرُ فَلَيْسَ بَعْدَكَ شَىْءٌ، وَاَنْتَ الظَّاهِرُ فَلَيْسَ فَوْقَكَ شَىْءٌ، وَاَنْتَ الْبَاطِنُ فَلَيْسَ دُوْنَكَ شَىْءٌ، اِقْضِ عَنَّا الدَّيْنَ وَاَغْنِنَا مِنَ الْفَقْرِ
Translation: O Allah, Lord of the heavens, Lord of the earth and Lord of the mighty Throne, our Lord and Lord of all things, Splitter of the seed and the date stone, Revealer of the Tawrat, the Injil and the Furqan, I seek refuge in You from the evil of every creature that You seize by the forelock. O Allah, You are the First and there is nothing before You; You are the Last and there is nothing after You. You are the Manifest and there is nothing above You; You are the Hidden and there is nothing beyond You. Settle our debt and spare us from poverty.
Benefit Text: Suhail reported that Abu Salih used to command us: When any one of you intends to go to sleep, he should lie on the bed on his right side and then say this dua.
Illustration: A sleeping figure beneath a soft blanket, a crescent moon and stars above, a gentle guardian-light hovering nearby.

Arabic: اَللّٰهُمَّ اِنِّيْۤ اَعُوْذُ بِوَجْهِكَ الْكَرِيْمِ، وَكَلِمَاتِكَ التَّآمَّاتِ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا اَنْتَ اٰخِذٌ بِنَاصِيَتِهٖ، اَللّٰهُمَّ اَنْتَ تَكْشِفُ الْمَغْرَمَ وَالْمَأْثَمَ، اَللّٰهُمَّ لَا يُهْزَمُ جُنْدُكَ وَلَا يُخْلَفُ وَعْدُكَ، وَلَا يَنْفَعُ ذَا الْجَدِّ مِنْكَ الْجَدُّ، سُبْحَانَكَ وَبِحَمْدِكَ
Translation: O Allah, I seek refuge in Your noble countenance and Your perfect words from the evil of all things that You seize by the forelock. O Allah, You remove debt and sin. O Allah, Your troops will not be defeated, nor Your promise will not be broken, nor can the richness of a rich man avail him anything before You, glory and praise be to You.
Benefit Text: Narrated Ali ibn AbuTalib (RA): The Messenger of Allah used to say when he lay down this dua.
Illustration: A sleeping figure beneath a soft blanket, a crescent moon and stars above, a gentle guardian-light hovering nearby.

Arabic: بِسْمِ اللهِ وَضَعْتُ جَنْبِيْ، اَللّٰهُمَّ اغْفِرْ لِيْ ذَنْبِيْ، وَاَخْسِئْ شَيْطَانِيْ، وَفُكَّ رِهَانِيْ، وَاجْعَلْنِيْ فِىْ النَّدِيِّ الْاَعْلٰى
Translation: In the name of Allah I lie down. O Allah, forgive me my sins, suppress my shaytan, ransom me and join me with the highest assembly.
Benefit Text: Narrated AbulAzhar al-Anmari (RA): When the Messenger of Allah went to his bed at night, he would say: in the name of Allah, I have laid down my side for Allah. O Allah! forgive me my sin, drive away my devil, free me from my responsibility, and place me in the highest assembly.
Illustration: A hand gently smoothing the surface of a moonlit bed, a soft white blanket folded back to receive a sleeper.

Arabic: اَلْحَمْدُ لِلّٰهِ الَّذِىْ كَفَانِيْ وَاٰوَانِيْ، وَاَطْعَمَنِيْ وَسَقَانِيْ، وَالَّذِىْ مَنَّ عَلَىَّ فَاَفْضَلَ، وَالَّذِىْ اَعْطَانِيْ فَاَجْزَلَ، اَلْحَمْدُ لِلّٰهِ عَلٰى كُلِّ حَالٍ، اَللّٰهُمَّ رَبَّ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ وَّمَلِيْكَهٗ وَاِلٰهَ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ، اَعُوْذُ بِكَ مِنَ النَّارِ
Translation: Praise be to Allah Who has given me sufficiency, has guarded me, given me food and drink, been most gracious to me, and given to me most lavishly. Praise be to Allah in every circumstance. O Allah! Lord and King of everything, God of everything, I seek refuge in Thee from Hell.
Benefit Text: Narrated Abdullah ibn Umar (RA): When the Messenger of Allah went to his bed, he would say this dua.
Illustration: A sleeping figure beneath a soft blanket, a crescent moon and stars above, a gentle guardian-light hovering nearby.

Arabic: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحْمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ
Translation: Recite the entire Surah As-Sajdah (Chapter 32 of the Qur'an, 30 verses) before sleep. Tap the button below to open the full Surah in the Qur'an Reader.
Benefit Text: Jabir (RA) said: The Prophet ﷺ would not sleep until he recited Tanzeel as-Sajdah (Surah As-Sajdah) and Tabarak (Surah Al-Mulk).
Illustration: Open Quran on Surah Al-Mulk under a starry night sky, a soft shield of light enveloping a sleeping form.

Arabic: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحْمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ
Translation: Recite the entire Surah Al-Mulk (Chapter 67 of the Qur'an, 30 verses) before sleep. Tap the button below to open the full Surah in the Qur'an Reader.
Benefit Text: Jabir (RA) said: The Prophet ﷺ would not sleep until he recited Tanzeel as-Sajdah (Surah As-Sajdah) and Tabarak (Surah Al-Mulk).
Illustration: Open Quran on Surah Al-Mulk under a starry night sky, a soft shield of light enveloping a sleeping form.

Arabic: رَبَّنَا آتِنَا فِي الدُّنْيَا حَسَنَةً وَفِي الْآخِرَةِ حَسَنَةً وَقِنَا عَذَابَ النَّارِ
Translation: “Our Lord! Grant us the good of this world and the Hereafter, and protect us from the torment of the Fire”
Benefit Text: Recited between the Yemeni Corner and the Black Stone during Tawaf.
Illustration: Close view of the Kaaba’s corner with hands gently raised in greeting toward the Black Stone, soft starlight overhead.

Arabic: رَبَّنَا أَفْرِغْ عَلَيْنَا صَبْرًا وَثَبِّتْ أَقْدَامَنَا وَانْصُرْنَا عَلَى الْقَوْمِ الْكَافِرِينَ
Translation: “Our Lord! Shower us with perseverance, make our steps firm, and give us victory over the disbelieving people”
Illustration: An open Quran on a wooden rehl with a soft gold ray rising from the page, hands gently cupped to receive it.

Arabic: رَبَّنَا لَا تُؤَاخِذْنَا إِنْ نَسِينَا أَوْ أَخْطَأْنَا، رَبَّنَا وَلَا تَحْمِلْ عَلَيْنَا إِصْرًا كَمَا حَمَلْتَهُ عَلَى الَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلِنَا، رَبَّنَا وَلَا تُحَمِّلْنَا مَا لَا طَاقَةَ لَنَا بِهِ، وَاعْفُ عَنَّا وَاغْفِرْ لَنَا وَارْحَمْنَا، أَنْتَ مَوْلَانَا فَانْصُرْنَا عَلَى الْقَوْمِ الْكَافِرِينَ
Translation: “Our Lord! Do not punish us if we forget or make a mistake. Our Lord! Do not place a burden on us like the one you placed on those before us. Our Lord! Do not burden us with what we cannot bear. Pardon us, forgive us, and have mercy on us. You are our ˹only˺ Guardian. So grant us victory over the disbelieving people”
Benefit Text: The closing verses of al-Baqarah; recited at night for protection.
Illustration: An open Quran on a wooden rehl with a soft gold ray rising from the page, hands gently cupped to receive it.

Arabic: رَبَّنَا لَا تُزِغْ قُلُوبَنَا بَعْدَ إِذْ هَدَيْتَنَا وَهَبْ لَنَا مِنْ لَدُنْكَ رَحْمَةً إِنَّكَ أَنْتَ الْوَهَّابُ
Translation: “Our Lord! Do not let our hearts deviate after you have guided us. Grant us Your mercy. You are indeed the Giver ˹of all bounties˺”
Benefit Text: A prayer for the heart to stay firm upon guidance.
Illustration: An open Quran on a wooden rehl with a soft gold ray rising from the page, hands gently cupped to receive it.

Arabic: رَبَّنَا إِنَّنَا آمَنَّا فَاغْفِرْ لَنَا ذُنُوبَنَا وَقِنَا عَذَابَ النَّارِ
Translation: “Our Lord! We have believed, so forgive our sins and protect us from the torment of the Fire”
Illustration: An open Quran on a wooden rehl with a soft gold ray rising from the page, hands gently cupped to receive it.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ مَالِكَ الْمُلْكِ تُؤْتِي الْمُلْكَ مَنْ تَشَاءُ وَتَنْزِعُ الْمُلْكَ مِمَّنْ تَشَاءُ وَتُعِزُّ مَنْ تَشَاءُ وَتُذِلُّ مَنْ تَشَاءُ بِيَدِكَ الْخَيْرُ إِنَّكَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ
Translation: “O Allah! Lord over all authorities! You give authority to whoever You please and remove it from who You please; You honour whoever You please and disgrace who You please—all good is in Your Hands. Surely You ˹alone˺ are Most Capable of everything”
Illustration: An open Quran on a wooden rehl with a soft gold ray rising from the page, hands gently cupped to receive it.

Arabic: رَبِّ هَبْ لِي مِنْ لَدُنْكَ ذُرِّيَّةً طَيِّبَةً إِنَّكَ سَمِيعُ الدُّعَاءِ
Translation: “My Lord! Grant me—by your grace—righteous offspring. You are certainly the Hearer of ˹all˺ prayers”
Benefit Text: The prayer of Zakariyya, longing for a child.
Illustration: A pair of cupped hands holding two small luminous seedlings, a gentle ray of light nurturing them from above.

Arabic: رَبَّنَا آمَنَّا بِمَا أَنْزَلْتَ وَاتَّبَعْنَا الرَّسُولَ فَاكْتُبْنَا مَعَ الشَّاهِدِينَ
Translation: “Our Lord! We believe in Your revelations and follow the messenger, so count us among those who bear witness”
Illustration: An open Quran on a wooden rehl with a soft gold ray rising from the page, hands gently cupped to receive it.

Arabic: رَبَّنَا اغْفِرْ لَنَا ذُنُوبَنَا وَإِسْرَافَنَا فِي أَمْرِنَا وَثَبِّتْ أَقْدَامَنَا وَانْصُرْنَا عَلَى الْقَوْمِ الْكَافِرِينَ
Translation: “Our Lord! Forgive our sins and excesses, make our steps firm, and grant us victory over the disbelieving people”
Illustration: An open Quran on a wooden rehl with a soft gold ray rising from the page, hands gently cupped to receive it.

Arabic: رَبَّنَا مَا خَلَقْتَ هَٰذَا بَاطِلًا سُبْحَانَكَ فَقِنَا عَذَابَ النَّارِ، رَبَّنَا إِنَّكَ مَنْ تُدْخِلِ النَّارَ فَقَدْ أَخْزَيْتَهُ وَمَا لِلظَّالِمِينَ مِنْ أَنْصَارٍ، رَبَّنَا إِنَّنَا سَمِعْنَا مُنَادِيًا يُنَادِي لِلْإِيمَانِ أَنْ آمِنُوا بِرَبِّكُمْ فَآمَنَّا، رَبَّنَا فَاغْفِرْ لَنَا ذُنُوبَنَا وَكَفِّرْ عَنَّا سَيِّئَاتِنَا وَتَوَفَّنَا مَعَ الْأَبْرَارِ، رَبَّنَا وَآتِنَا مَا وَعَدْتَنَا عَلَى رُسُلِكَ وَلَا تُخْزِنَا يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ إِنَّكَ لَا تُخْلِفُ الْمِيعَادَ
Translation: “Our Lord! You have not created ˹all of˺ this without purpose. Glory be to You! Protect us from the torment of the Fire.Our Lord! Indeed, those You commit to the Fire will be ˹completely˺ disgraced! And the wrongdoers will have no helpers.Our Lord! We have heard the caller to ˹true˺ belief, ˹proclaiming,˺ ‘Believe in your Lord ˹alone˺,’ so we believed. Our Lord! Forgive our sins, absolve us of our misdeeds, and join us with the virtuous when we die‎.Our Lord! Grant us what You have promised us through Your messengers and do not put us to shame on Judgment Day—for certainly You never fail in Your promise”
Benefit Text: From the closing verses of Aal ‘Imran, on the signs of creation.
Illustration: An open Quran on a wooden rehl with a soft gold ray rising from the page, hands gently cupped to receive it.

Arabic: رَبَّنَا آمَنَّا فَاكْتُبْنَا مَعَ الشَّاهِدِينَ
Translation: “Our Lord! We believe, so count us among the witnesses”
Illustration: An open Quran on a wooden rehl with a soft gold ray rising from the page, hands gently cupped to receive it.

Arabic: رَبَّنَا ظَلَمْنَا أَنْفُسَنَا وَإِنْ لَمْ تَغْفِرْ لَنَا وَتَرْحَمْنَا لَنَكُونَنَّ مِنَ الْخَاسِرِينَ
Translation: “Our Lord! We have wronged ourselves. If You do not forgive us and have mercy on us, we will certainly be losers”
Benefit Text: The words of Adam and his wife after their slip.
Illustration: An open Quran on a wooden rehl with a soft gold ray rising from the page, hands gently cupped to receive it.

Arabic: رَبَّنَا لَا تَجْعَلْنَا مَعَ الْقَوْمِ الظَّالِمِينَ
Translation: “Our Lord! Do not join us with the wrongdoing people”
Illustration: An open Quran on a wooden rehl with a soft gold ray rising from the page, hands gently cupped to receive it.

Arabic: رَبَّنَا لَا تَجْعَلْنَا فِتْنَةً لِلْقَوْمِ الظَّالِمِينَ
Translation: “Our Lord! Do not subject us to the persecution of the oppressive people”
Benefit Text: The prayer of the believers among the people of Musa.
Illustration: An open Quran on a wooden rehl with a soft gold ray rising from the page, hands gently cupped to receive it.

Arabic: رَبِّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ أَنْ أَسْأَلَكَ مَا لَيْسَ لِي بِهِ عِلْمٌ وَإِلَّا تَغْفِرْ لِي وَتَرْحَمْنِي أَكُنْ مِنَ الْخَاسِرِينَ
Translation: “My Lord, I seek refuge in You from asking You about what I have no knowledge of, and unless You forgive me and have mercy on me, I will be one of the losers”
Benefit Text: The prayer of Nuh after the Flood.
Illustration: An open Quran on a wooden rehl with a soft gold ray rising from the page, hands gently cupped to receive it.

Arabic: رَبِّ اجْعَلْ هَٰذَا الْبَلَدَ آمِنًا وَاجْنُبْنِي وَبَنِيَّ أَنْ نَعْبُدَ الْأَصْنَامَ
Translation: “My Lord! Make this city ˹of Mecca˺ secure, and keep me and my children away from the worship of idols”
Benefit Text: From the prayers of Ibrahim for Mecca and his descendants.
Illustration: An open Quran on a wooden rehl with a soft gold ray rising from the page, hands gently cupped to receive it.

Arabic: رَبِّ اجْعَلْنِي مُقِيمَ الصَّلَاةِ وَمِنْ ذُرِّيَّتِي رَبَّنَا وَتَقَبَّلْ دُعَاءِ
Translation: “My Lord! Make me and those ˹believers˺ of my descendants keep up prayer. Our Lord! Accept my prayers”
Illustration: An open Quran on a wooden rehl with a soft gold ray rising from the page, hands gently cupped to receive it.

Arabic: رَبَّنَا اغْفِرْ لِي وَلِوَالِدَيَّ وَلِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ يَوْمَ يَقُومُ الْحِسَابُ
Translation: “Our Lord! Forgive me, my parents, and the believers on the Day when the judgment will come to pass”
Illustration: An open Quran on a wooden rehl with a soft gold ray rising from the page, hands gently cupped to receive it.

Arabic: رَبِّ ارْحَمْهُمَا كَمَا رَبَّيَانِي صَغِيرًا
Translation: “My Lord! Be merciful to them as they raised me when I was young”
Benefit Text: The Qur’an’s prayer for one’s parents.
Illustration: An open Quran on a wooden rehl with a soft gold ray rising from the page, hands gently cupped to receive it.

Arabic: رَبِّ أَدْخِلْنِي مُدْخَلَ صِدْقٍ وَأَخْرِجْنِي مُخْرَجَ صِدْقٍ وَاجْعَلْ لِي مِنْ لَدُنْكَ سُلْطَانًا نَصِيرًا
Translation: “My Lord! Grant me an honourable entrance and an honourable exit and give me a supporting authority from Yourself”
Illustration: An open Quran on a wooden rehl with a soft gold ray rising from the page, hands gently cupped to receive it.

Arabic: رَبَّنَا آتِنَا مِنْ لَدُنْكَ رَحْمَةً وَهَيِّئْ لَنَا مِنْ أَمْرِنَا رَشَدًا
Translation: “Our Lord! Grant us mercy from Yourself and guide us rightly through our ordeal”
Benefit Text: The prayer of the young believers of the Cave.
Illustration: An open Quran on a wooden rehl with a soft gold ray rising from the page, hands gently cupped to receive it.

Arabic: رَبِّ زِدْنِي عِلْمًا
Translation: “My Lord! Increase me in knowledge”
Benefit Text: A prayer for increase in beneficial knowledge.
Illustration: An open Quran on a wooden rehl with a soft gold ray rising from the page, hands gently cupped to receive it.

Arabic: لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ سُبْحَانَكَ إِنِّي كُنْتُ مِنَ الظَّالِمِينَ
Translation: “There is no god ˹worthy of worship˺ except You. Glory be to You! I have certainly done wrong”
Benefit Text: The prayer of Yunus (Dhu’n-Nun) from within the whale.
Illustration: A small lone figure within softly-lit deep blue water, a single ray of mercy piercing the dark from above.

Arabic: رَبِّ لَا تَذَرْنِي فَرْدًا وَأَنْتَ خَيْرُ الْوَارِثِينَ
Translation: “My Lord! Do not leave me childless, though You are the Best of Successors”
Benefit Text: The prayer of Zakariyya for offspring.
Illustration: An open Quran on a wooden rehl with a soft gold ray rising from the page, hands gently cupped to receive it.

Arabic: رَبِّ أَنْزِلْنِي مُنْزَلًا مُبَارَكًا وَأَنْتَ خَيْرُ الْمُنْزِلِينَ
Translation: “My Lord! Allow me a blessed landing, for You are the best accommodator”
Benefit Text: The prayer of Nuh on boarding the Ark.
Illustration: An open Quran on a wooden rehl with a soft gold ray rising from the page, hands gently cupped to receive it.

Arabic: رَبِّ أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ هَمَزَاتِ الشَّيَاطِينِ وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ رَبِّ أَنْ يَحْضُرُونِ
Translation: “My Lord! I seek refuge in You from the temptations of the devils And I seek refuge in You, my Lord, that they ˹even˺ come near me”
Illustration: An open Quran on a wooden rehl with a soft gold ray rising from the page, hands gently cupped to receive it.

Arabic: رَبَّنَا آمَنَّا فَاغْفِرْ لَنَا وَارْحَمْنَا وَأَنْتَ خَيْرُ الرَّاحِمِينَ
Translation: “Our Lord! We have believed, so forgive us and have mercy on us, for You are the best of those who show mercy”
Illustration: An open Quran on a wooden rehl with a soft gold ray rising from the page, hands gently cupped to receive it.

Arabic: رَبِّ اغْفِرْ وَارْحَمْ وَأَنْتَ خَيْرُ الرَّاحِمِينَ
Translation: “My Lord! Forgive and have mercy, for You are the best of those who show mercy”
Illustration: An open Quran on a wooden rehl with a soft gold ray rising from the page, hands gently cupped to receive it.

Arabic: رَبِّ أَوْزِعْنِي أَنْ أَشْكُرَ نِعْمَتَكَ الَّتِي أَنْعَمْتَ عَلَيَّ وَعَلَى وَالِدَيَّ وَأَنْ أَعْمَلَ صَالِحًا تَرْضَاهُ وَأَدْخِلْنِي بِرَحْمَتِكَ فِي عِبَادِكَ الصَّالِحِينَ
Translation: “My Lord! Inspire me to ˹always˺ be thankful for Your favours which You have blessed me and my parents with, and to do good deeds that please you. Admit me, by Your mercy, into ˹the company of˺ Your righteous servants”
Benefit Text: The prayer of Sulayman in gratitude.
Illustration: An open Quran on a wooden rehl with a soft gold ray rising from the page, hands gently cupped to receive it.

Arabic: رَبِّ إِنِّي ظَلَمْتُ نَفْسِي فَاغْفِرْ لِي
Translation: “My Lord! I have definitely wronged my soul, so forgive me.”
Benefit Text: The prayer of Musa after his error in Egypt.
Illustration: An open Quran on a wooden rehl with a soft gold ray rising from the page, hands gently cupped to receive it.

Arabic: رَبِّ بِمَا أَنْعَمْتَ عَلَيَّ فَلَنْ أَكُونَ ظَهِيرًا لِلْمُجْرِمِينَ
Translation: “My Lord! For all Your favours upon me, I will never side with the wicked”
Illustration: An open Quran on a wooden rehl with a soft gold ray rising from the page, hands gently cupped to receive it.

Arabic: رَبِّ نَجِّنِي مِنَ الْقَوْمِ الظَّالِمِينَ
Translation: “My Lord! Deliver me from the wrongdoing people”
Illustration: An open Quran on a wooden rehl with a soft gold ray rising from the page, hands gently cupped to receive it.

Arabic: رَبِّ إِنِّي لِمَا أَنْزَلْتَ إِلَيَّ مِنْ خَيْرٍ فَقِيرٌ
Translation: “My Lord! I am truly in ˹desperate˺ need of whatever provision You may have in store for me”
Benefit Text: The prayer of Musa, alone and in need in Madyan.
Illustration: An open Quran on a wooden rehl with a soft gold ray rising from the page, hands gently cupped to receive it.

Arabic: رَبِّ انْصُرْنِي عَلَى الْقَوْمِ الْمُفْسِدِينَ
Translation: “My Lord! Help me against the people of corruption”
Illustration: An open Quran on a wooden rehl with a soft gold ray rising from the page, hands gently cupped to receive it.

Arabic: رَبَّنَا اصْرِفْ عَنَّا عَذَابَ جَهَنَّمَ إِنَّ عَذَابَهَا كَانَ غَرَامًا
Translation: “Our Lord! Keep the punishment of Hell away from us, for its punishment is indeed unrelenting”
Illustration: An open Quran on a wooden rehl with a soft gold ray rising from the page, hands gently cupped to receive it.

Arabic: رَبَّنَا هَبْ لَنَا مِنْ أَزْوَاجِنَا وَذُرِّيَّاتِنَا قُرَّةَ أَعْيُنٍ وَاجْعَلْنَا لِلْمُتَّقِينَ إِمَامًا
Translation: “Our Lord! Bless us with ˹pious˺ spouses and offspring who will be the joy of our hearts, and make us models for the righteous”
Benefit Text: The prayer of the servants of the Most Merciful.
Illustration: Two silhouettes side by side beneath a soft canopy of light, small star-blossoms drifting gently down between them.

Arabic: رَبَّنَا وَسِعْتَ كُلَّ شَيْءٍ رَحْمَةً وَعِلْمًا فَاغْفِرْ لِلَّذِينَ تَابُوا وَاتَّبَعُوا سَبِيلَكَ وَقِهِمْ عَذَابَ الْجَحِيمِ، رَبَّنَا وَأَدْخِلْهُمْ جَنَّاتِ عَدْنٍ الَّتِي وَعَدْتَهُمْ وَمَنْ صَلَحَ مِنْ آبَائِهِمْ وَأَزْوَاجِهِمْ وَذُرِّيَّاتِهِمْ إِنَّكَ أَنْتَ الْعَزِيزُ الْحَكِيمُ
Translation: “Our Lord! You encompass everything in ˹Your˺ mercy and knowledge. So forgive those who repent and follow Your Way, and protect them from the torment of the Hellfire”
Illustration: An open Quran on a wooden rehl with a soft gold ray rising from the page, hands gently cupped to receive it.

Arabic: رَبِّ أَوْزِعْنِي أَنْ أَشْكُرَ نِعْمَتَكَ الَّتِي أَنْعَمْتَ عَلَيَّ وَعَلَى وَالِدَيَّ وَأَنْ أَعْمَلَ صَالِحًا تَرْضَاهُ وَأَصْلِحْ لِي فِي ذُرِّيَّتِي إِنِّي تُبْتُ إِلَيْكَ وَإِنِّي مِنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ
Translation: “My Lord! Inspire me to ˹always˺ be thankful for Your favours which You blessed me and my parents with, and to do good deeds that please You. And instil righteousness in my offspring. I truly repent to You, and I truly submit ˹to Your Will˺”
Illustration: Hands cupped in repentance, soft drops of forgiving light falling from above onto the open palms.

Arabic: رَبِّ هَبْ لِي مِنَ الصَّالِحِينَ
Translation: “My Lord! Bless me with righteous offspring”
Benefit Text: The prayer of Ibrahim for righteous offspring.
Illustration: A pair of cupped hands holding two small luminous seedlings, a gentle ray of light nurturing them from above.

Arabic: رَبَّنَا اغْفِرْ لَنَا وَلِإِخْوَانِنَا الَّذِينَ سَبَقُونَا بِالْإِيمَانِ وَلَا تَجْعَلْ فِي قُلُوبِنَا غِلًّا لِلَّذِينَ آمَنُوا رَبَّنَا إِنَّكَ رَؤُوفٌ رَحِيمٌ
Translation: “Our Lord! Forgive us and our fellow believers who preceded us in faith, and do not allow bitterness into our hearts towards those who believe. Our Lord! Indeed, You are Ever Gracious, Most Merciful”
Illustration: An open Quran on a wooden rehl with a soft gold ray rising from the page, hands gently cupped to receive it.

Arabic: رَبَّنَا عَلَيْكَ تَوَكَّلْنَا وَإِلَيْكَ أَنَبْنَا وَإِلَيْكَ الْمَصِيرُ، رَبَّنَا لَا تَجْعَلْنَا فِتْنَةً لِلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا وَاغْفِرْ لَنَا رَبَّنَا إِنَّكَ أَنْتَ الْعَزِيزُ الْحَكِيمُ
Translation: “Our Lord! In You we trust. And to You we ˹always˺ turn. And to You is the final return”
Illustration: An open Quran on a wooden rehl with a soft gold ray rising from the page, hands gently cupped to receive it.

Arabic: رَبِّ اغْفِرْ لِي وَلِوَالِدَيَّ وَلِمَنْ دَخَلَ بَيْتِيَ مُؤْمِنًا وَلِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَالْمُؤْمِنَاتِ وَلَا تَزِدِ الظَّالِمِينَ إِلَّا تَبَارًا
Translation: “My Lord! Forgive me, my parents, and whoever enters my house in faith, and ˹all˺ believing men and women. And increase the wrongdoers only in destruction”
Benefit Text: The prayer of Nuh for the believers.
Illustration: An open Quran on a wooden rehl with a soft gold ray rising from the page, hands gently cupped to receive it.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ آتِنَا فِي الدُّنْيَا حَسَنَةً وَفِي الْآخِرَةِ حَسَنَةً وَقِنَا عَذَابَ النَّارِ
Translation: “O Allah! Give us in this world that which is good and in the Hereafter that which is good, and save us from the torment of the Fire.”
Illustration: A pair of open hands raised in quiet supplication beneath a soft halo of light, prayer beads resting nearby.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ فِتْنَةِ النَّارِ، وَفِتْنَةِ الْقَبْرِ، وَعَذَابِ الْقَبْرِ، وَشَرِّ فِتْنَةِ الْغِنَى، وَشَرِّ فِتْنَةِ الْفَقْرِ، اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّ فِتْنَةِ الْمَسِيحِ الدَّجَّالِ، اللَّهُمَّ اغْسِلْ قَلْبِي بِمَاءِ الثَّلْجِ وَالْبَرَدِ، وَنَقِّ قَلْبِي مِنَ الْخَطَايَا كَمَا نَقَّيْتَ الثَّوْبَ الْأَبْيَضَ مِنَ الدَّنَسِ، وَبَاعِدْ بَيْنِي وَبَيْنَ خَطَايَايَ كَمَا بَاعَدْتَ بَيْنَ الْمَشْرِقِ وَالْمَغْرِبِ، اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنَ الْكَسَلِ وَالْمَأْثَمِ وَالْمَغْرَمِ
Translation: “O Allah! I ask refuge with You from the torment of the Fire, and ordeal in the grave, and the punishment of the grave, and the evil (brought by) affluence and poverty. O Allah! I ask refuge with You from the trials of the Masihud Dajjal (False Messiah). O Allah, cleanse my heart with snow and cool water, and purify my heart from misdeeds as You purify the white cloth from dirt, and keep me away from sinful acts as You keep the East far away from the West. O Allah! I ask refuge with You from being lazy, sinful and having liabilities (due to debts).”
Illustration: A simple white-shrouded form at peace under a soft starlit sky, a gentle column of mercy-light descending from above.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنَ الْعَجْزِ وَالْكَسَلِ، وَالْجُبْنِ وَالْهَرَمِ وَالْبُخْلِ، وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ عَذَابِ الْقَبْرِ، وَمِنْ فِتْنَةِ الْمَحْيَا وَالْمَمَاتِ
Translation: “O Allah! I ask refuge with You from weakness, indolence, cowardliness, senility and miserliness. I (also) ask refuge with You from punishment of the grave, and from the trials of life and the ordeals of death.”
Illustration: A simple white-shrouded form at peace under a soft starlit sky, a gentle column of mercy-light descending from above.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ جَهْدِ الْبَلَاءِ، وَدَرَكِ الشَّقَاءِ، وَسُوءِ الْقَضَاءِ، وَشَمَاتَةِ الْأَعْدَاءِ
Translation: “O Allah! I ask refuge with You against any severe trial and bad misfortune, and adverse judgment and satire of the enemies.”
Illustration: A pair of open hands raised in quiet supplication beneath a soft halo of light, prayer beads resting nearby.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ أَصْلِحْ لِي دِينِي الَّذِي هُوَ عِصْمَةُ أَمْرِي، وَأَصْلِحْ لِي دُنْيَايَ الَّتِي فِيهَا مَعَاشِي، وَأَصْلِحْ لِي آخِرَتِي الَّتِي فِيهَا مَعَادِي، وَاجْعَلِ الْحَيَاةَ زِيَادَةً لِي فِي كُلِّ خَيْرٍ، وَاجْعَلِ الْمَوْتَ رَاحَةً لِي مِنْ كُلِّ شَرٍّ
Translation: “O Allah! Make upright my religion which is the basis of my activity, make my world suited for my best subsistence, and the Hereafter my best Final Destination, and make life easy for me (to earn) all good and make death for me as relief from all evils.”
Illustration: A pair of open hands raised in quiet supplication beneath a soft halo of light, prayer beads resting nearby.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ الْهُدَى وَالتُّقَى وَالْعَفَافَ وَالْغِنَى
Translation: “O Allah! I am asking You Guidance, Piety, Virtue and Wealth.”
Illustration: A pair of open hands raised in quiet supplication beneath a soft halo of light, prayer beads resting nearby.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنَ الْعَجْزِ وَالْكَسَلِ، وَالْجُبْنِ وَالْبُخْلِ وَالْهَرَمِ، وَعَذَابِ الْقَبْرِ، اللَّهُمَّ آتِ نَفْسِي تَقْوَاهَا، وَزَكِّهَا أَنْتَ خَيْرُ مَنْ زَكَّاهَا، أَنْتَ وَلِيُّهَا وَمَوْلَاهَا، اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ عِلْمٍ لَا يَنْفَعُ، وَمِنْ قَلْبٍ لَا يَخْشَعُ، وَمِنْ نَفْسٍ لَا تَشْبَعُ، وَمِنْ دَعْوَةٍ لَا يُسْتَجَابُ لَهَا
Translation: “O Allah! I ask refuge with You from weakness, indolence, cowardliness, senility and miserliness. I (also) ask refuge with You from punishment of the grave. O Allah! Give myself its piety (fear to You), and purify it as You are the Best of those who purify, You are its Protector and Master. O Allah! I ask refuge with You from knowledge that which is worthless, from heart that which is not subservient (to You), from self that which is unsatisfied, and from invocation that which is not accepted.”
Illustration: A simple white-shrouded form at peace under a soft starlit sky, a gentle column of mercy-light descending from above.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ اهْدِنِي وَسَدِّدْنِي، اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ الْهُدَى وَالسَّدَادَ
Translation: “O Allah! Guide me and direct me. O Allah! I ask You Guidance and Direction.”
Illustration: A pair of open hands raised in quiet supplication beneath a soft halo of light, prayer beads resting nearby.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ زَوَالِ نِعْمَتِكَ، وَتَحَوُّلِ عَافِيَتِكَ، وَفُجَاءَةِ نِقْمَتِكَ، وَجَمِيعِ سَخَطِكَ
Translation: “O Allah! I ask refuge with You from the cessation of Your Blessing, and Your Protection (of me from harm), and Your sudden Wrath and all that displeased You.”
Illustration: A pair of open hands raised in quiet supplication beneath a soft halo of light, prayer beads resting nearby.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ أَكْثِرْ مَالِي وَوَلَدِي، وَبَارِكْ لِي فِيمَا أَعْطَيْتَنِي، وَأَطِلْ حَيَاتِي عَلَى طَاعَتِكَ، وَأَحْسِنْ عَمَلِي وَاغْفِرْ لِي
Translation: “O Allah! Increase my wealth and (provide me) more children, bless for me all that You give me (wealth, children, knowledge, etc.), prolong my life in obeying You, (help me) excel in my (Islamic) activities, and forgive me.”
Illustration: A pair of open hands raised in quiet supplication beneath a soft halo of light, prayer beads resting nearby.

Arabic: لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ الْعَظِيمُ الْحَلِيمُ، لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ رَبُّ الْعَرْشِ الْعَظِيمِ، لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ رَبُّ السَّمَوَاتِ وَرَبُّ الْأَرْضِ وَرَبُّ الْعَرْشِ الْكَرِيمِ
Translation: “None has the right to be worshipped but Allah, the Great Forbearer. None has the right to be worshipped but Allah, Lord of the Great Throne. None has the right to be worshipped but Allah, Lord of the Heavens and the Earth and Lord of the Noble Throne.”
Illustration: Calligraphy of the Kalimah glowing in the sky, chains of slavery breaking and falling toward the earth below.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ رَحْمَتَكَ أَرْجُو، فَلَا تَكِلْنِي إِلَى نَفْسِي طَرْفَةَ عَيْنٍ، وَأَصْلِحْ لِي شَأْنِي كُلَّهُ، لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ
Translation: “O Allah! Your Mercy is what I am yearning for, so (please) don't stop guiding myself even in a blink of an eye, set right for me all of my actions, none has the right to be worshipped except You (O Allah).”
Illustration: Calligraphy of the Kalimah glowing in the sky, chains of slavery breaking and falling toward the earth below.

Arabic: لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ سُبْحَانَكَ إِنِّي كُنْتُ مِنَ الظَّالِمِينَ
Translation: “La ilaha illa anta [none has the right to be worshipped but You (O Allah)], Glorified (and Exalted) be You [above all that (evil) they associate with You]! Truly, I have been of the wrong-doers.”
Illustration: Calligraphy of the Kalimah glowing in the sky, chains of slavery breaking and falling toward the earth below.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي عَبْدُكَ ابْنُ عَبْدِكَ ابْنُ أَمَتِكَ، نَاصِيَتِي بِيَدِكَ، مَاضٍ فِيَّ حُكْمُكَ، عَدْلٌ فِيَّ قَضَاؤُكَ، أَسْأَلُكَ بِكُلِّ اسْمٍ هُوَ لَكَ، سَمَّيْتَ بِهِ نَفْسَكَ، أَوْ أَنْزَلْتَهُ فِي كِتَابِكَ، أَوْ عَلَّمْتَهُ أَحَدًا مِنْ خَلْقِكَ، أَوِ اسْتَأْثَرْتَ بِهِ فِي عِلْمِ الْغَيْبِ عِنْدَكَ، أَنْ تَجْعَلَ الْقُرْآنَ رَبِيعَ قَلْبِي، وَنُورَ صَدْرِي، وَجَلَاءَ حُزْنِي، وَذَهَابَ هَمِّي
Translation: “O Allah! I am Your slave, son of Your male and female slaves, my thoughts are in Your Hand, Your Command I must endeavor to (fully and) continuously fulfill, You are Just in Your Commands (to me). I am asking You with all Your Names that which You named for Yourself, or revealed in Your Book, or You taught to one of Your creatures, or that which You keep in the unseen knowledge with You; to make the Qur'an the life of my heart, the light of my chest, reliever of my grief, and remover of my worries.”
Illustration: A pair of open hands raised in quiet supplication beneath a soft halo of light, prayer beads resting nearby.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ مُصَرِّفَ الْقُلُوبِ، صَرِّفْ قُلُوبَنَا عَلَى طَاعَتِكَ
Translation: “O Allah! (He Who) guides the hearts, (please) guide our hearts to obey You.”
Illustration: A pair of open hands raised in quiet supplication beneath a soft halo of light, prayer beads resting nearby.

Arabic: يَا مُقَلِّبَ الْقُلُوبِ، ثَبِّتْ قَلْبِي عَلَى دِينِكَ
Translation: “O (He Who) alters the hearts; make firm my heart upon Your religion.”
Illustration: A small glowing heart shielded by a translucent dome of light, dark whispering wisps held back at its surface.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ الْعَافِيَةَ فِي الدُّنْيَا وَالْآخِرَةِ
Translation: “O Allah! Grant me good well-being here on Earth and in the Hereafter.”
Illustration: A pair of open hands raised in quiet supplication beneath a soft halo of light, prayer beads resting nearby.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ أَحْسِنْ عَاقِبَتَنَا فِي الْأُمُورِ كُلِّهَا، وَأَجِرْنَا مِنْ خِزْيِ الدُّنْيَا وَعَذَابِ الْقَبْرِ
Translation: “O Allah! Give us good rewards for all our deeds; save us from the disgrace of this life and from the punishment of the grave.”
Illustration: A simple white-shrouded form at peace under a soft starlit sky, a gentle column of mercy-light descending from above.

Arabic: رَبِّ أَعِنِّي وَلَا تُعِنْ عَلَيَّ، وَانْصُرْنِي وَلَا تَنْصُرْ عَلَيَّ، وَامْكُرْ لِي وَلَا تَمْكُرْ عَلَيَّ، اجْعَلْنِي لَكَ شَكَّارًا، لَكَ ذَكَّارًا، لَكَ رَهَّابًا، لَكَ مِطْوَاعًا، إِلَيْكَ مُخْبِتًا أَوَّاهًا مُنِيبًا، رَبِّ تَقَبَّلْ تَوْبَتِي، وَاغْسِلْ حَوْبَتِي، وَأَجِبْ دَعْوَتِي، وَثَبِّتْ حُجَّتِي، وَاهْدِ قَلْبِي، وَسَدِّدْ لِسَانِي، وَاسْلُلْ سَخِيمَةَ قَلْبِي
Translation: “Lord! Help me to be mindful of good deeds and not of bad ones, provide me victory for good and not for bad, give me intellect for good not for bad, make me thankful to You (O Allah), praying to You (Alone), fearing You (Alone), obeying You (Alone), submissive to You (O Allah), and penitent to You (O Allah). Accept my repentance, purify me, answer my prayer, establish me firmly (in Your religion), guide my heart, direct my tongue (to say Right), and eliminate the black spots from my heart.”
Illustration: A pair of open hands raised in quiet supplication beneath a soft halo of light, prayer beads resting nearby.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّ سَمْعِي، وَمِنْ شَرِّ بَصَرِي، وَمِنْ شَرِّ لِسَانِي، وَمِنْ شَرِّ قَلْبِي، وَمِنْ شَرِّ مَنِيِّي
Translation: “O Allah! I ask refuge with You from the badness of my hearing, from the immorality of my vision, from the ruthlessness of my word, from the wickedness of my heart and from impiety of my semen.”
Illustration: A pair of open hands raised in quiet supplication beneath a soft halo of light, prayer beads resting nearby.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنَّكَ عَفُوٌّ كَرِيمٌ تُحِبُّ الْعَفْوَ فَاعْفُ عَنِّي
Translation: “O Allah! You are the Forgiver, the Generous; You love those who repent, so (please) forgive me.”
Illustration: A pair of open hands raised in quiet supplication beneath a soft halo of light, prayer beads resting nearby.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ فِعْلَ الْخَيْرَاتِ، وَتَرْكَ الْمُنْكَرَاتِ، وَحُبَّ الْمَسَاكِينِ، وَأَنْ تَغْفِرَ لِي وَتَرْحَمَنِي، وَإِذَا أَرَدْتَ فِتْنَةَ قَوْمٍ فَتَوَفَّنِي غَيْرَ مَفْتُونٍ، وَأَسْأَلُكَ حُبَّكَ، وَحُبَّ مَنْ يُحِبُّكَ، وَحُبَّ عَمَلٍ يُقَرِّبُنِي إِلَى حُبِّكَ
Translation: “O Allah! I ask You to let me do the good deeds and leave the forbidden ones, and love the poor, and forgive me and have mercy on me; if You want to destroy the people, (please) take my life (in order) not to be included with them. I ask Your Love (O Allah) and the love of those whom You loved, and to love the deeds that will bring me near to Your Love.”
Illustration: A pair of open hands raised in quiet supplication beneath a soft halo of light, prayer beads resting nearby.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ مِنَ الْخَيْرِ كُلِّهِ عَاجِلِهِ وَآجِلِهِ، مَا عَلِمْتُ مِنْهُ وَمَا لَمْ أَعْلَمْ، وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنَ الشَّرِّ كُلِّهِ عَاجِلِهِ وَآجِلِهِ، مَا عَلِمْتُ مِنْهُ وَمَا لَمْ أَعْلَمْ، اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ مِنْ خَيْرِ مَا سَأَلَكَ عَبْدُكَ وَنَبِيُّكَ، وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا اسْتَعَاذَ مِنْهُ عَبْدُكَ وَنَبِيُّكَ، اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ الْجَنَّةَ وَمَا قَرَّبَ إِلَيْهَا مِنْ قَوْلٍ أَوْ عَمَلٍ، وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنَ النَّارِ وَمَا قَرَّبَ إِلَيْهَا مِنْ قَوْلٍ أَوْ عَمَلٍ، وَأَسْأَلُكَ أَنْ تَجْعَلَ كُلَّ قَضَاءٍ قَضَيْتَهُ لِي خَيْرًا
Translation: “O Allah! I seek from You every goodness; both the immediate and the latter and which I would know or would not know. I ask refuge with You against every evil, both the immediate and the latter and which I would know or would not know. I ask from You the best which had been sought by Your slave and Prophet (pbuh), and I ask refuge with You against the evil of all such things from which protection had been sought by Your slave and Prophet (pbuh). O Allah! I ask You the Garden (Paradise) and any such deed or word which may bring me closer to it. I ask refuge with You against the Hell-Fire and from any such deed or word which may bring me closer to it, and I ask You (O Allah) to make good every fate You have destined for me.”
Illustration: A pair of open hands raised in quiet supplication beneath a soft halo of light, prayer beads resting nearby.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ احْفَظْنِي بِالْإِسْلَامِ قَائِمًا، وَاحْفَظْنِي بِالْإِسْلَامِ قَاعِدًا، وَاحْفَظْنِي بِالْإِسْلَامِ رَاقِدًا، وَلَا تُشْمِتْ بِي عَدُوًّا وَلَا حَاسِدًا، اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ مِنْ كُلِّ خَيْرٍ خَزَائِنُهُ بِيَدِكَ، وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ كُلِّ شَرٍّ خَزَائِنُهُ بِيَدِكَ
Translation: “O Allah! Protect me standing in Islam, protect me sitting in Islam, and protect me lying down in Islam; and don't allow enemy and envier to rejoice over my grief. O Allah! I ask You all that which is good that lay by Your Hand, and I ask refuge with You from all that which is bad that lay by Your Hand.”
Illustration: A pair of open hands raised in quiet supplication beneath a soft halo of light, prayer beads resting nearby.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنَ الْجُبْنِ، وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنَ الْبُخْلِ، وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ أَنْ أُرَدَّ إِلَى أَرْذَلِ الْعُمُرِ، وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ فِتْنَةِ الدُّنْيَا وَعَذَابِ الْقَبْرِ
Translation: “O Allah! I ask refuge with You from cowardice; and I ask refuge with You from miserliness; and I ask refuge with You from being sent back to geriatric old age; and I ask refuge with You from the Fitnah (trials and affliction etc) of this world and the punishment in the grave.”
Illustration: A simple white-shrouded form at peace under a soft starlit sky, a gentle column of mercy-light descending from above.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ اغْفِرْ لِي خَطِيئَتِي، وَجَهْلِي، وَإِسْرَافِي فِي أَمْرِي، وَمَا أَنْتَ أَعْلَمُ بِهِ مِنِّي، اللَّهُمَّ اغْفِرْ لِي هَزْلِي وَجِدِّي، وَخَطَئِي وَعَمْدِي، وَكُلُّ ذَلِكَ عِنْدِي
Translation: “O Allah! Forgive my mistakes, my ignorance, and my exceeding the limit (i.e. my great sins) and forgive whatever, You know better than I; O Allah! Forgive the wrong I have done jokingly or seriously, and forgive my accidental and intentional errors, all that is present in me.”
Illustration: A pair of open hands raised in quiet supplication beneath a soft halo of light, prayer beads resting nearby.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي ظَلَمْتُ نَفْسِي ظُلْمًا كَثِيرًا، وَلَا يَغْفِرُ الذُّنُوبَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ، فَاغْفِرْ لِي مَغْفِرَةً مِنْ عِنْدِكَ، وَارْحَمْنِي إِنَّكَ أَنْتَ الْغَفُورُ الرَّحِيمُ
Translation: “O Allah! I corrupted myself a lot; You (Alone) forgive (all) sins, so (please) forgive me with forgiveness from You, and have Mercy on me, You are indeed the Most Forgiving the Most Merciful.”
Illustration: A pair of open hands raised in quiet supplication beneath a soft halo of light, prayer beads resting nearby.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ لَكَ أَسْلَمْتُ، وَبِكَ آمَنْتُ، وَعَلَيْكَ تَوَكَّلْتُ، وَإِلَيْكَ أَنَبْتُ، وَبِكَ خَاصَمْتُ، اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِعِزَّتِكَ، لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ أَنْ تُضِلَّنِي، أَنْتَ الْحَيُّ الَّذِي لَا يَمُوتُ، وَالْجِنُّ وَالْإِنْسُ يَمُوتُونَ
Translation: “O Allah! I submit myself to You, and I believe in You, and I depend on You, and I repent to You, and return to You, and to You I leave the judgment. I seek refuge (with You) by Your Might, no one has the right to be worshipped but You, against Your misguidance; You are the Ever Living One Who dies not, while the jinn and the human beings die.”
Illustration: Hands cupped in repentance, soft drops of forgiving light falling from above onto the open palms.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنَّا نَسْأَلُكَ مُوجِبَاتِ رَحْمَتِكَ، وَعَزَائِمَ مَغْفِرَتِكَ، وَالسَّلَامَةَ مِنْ كُلِّ إِثْمٍ، وَالْغَنِيمَةَ مِنْ كُلِّ بِرٍّ، وَالْفَوْزَ بِالْجَنَّةِ، وَالنَّجَاةَ مِنَ النَّارِ
Translation: “O Allah! We ask the exigencies of Your Mercy, the comforts of Your Forgiveness, and security from all sins, and prizes from all devotion, and reward of Paradise, and deliverance from the Fire.”
Illustration: A pair of open hands raised in quiet supplication beneath a soft halo of light, prayer beads resting nearby.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا عَلِمْتُ، وَمِنْ شَرِّ مَا لَمْ أَعْلَمْ
Translation: “O Allah! I ask refuge with You from the evil of (all) that which I know and from the evil of (all) that which I know not.”
Illustration: A pair of open hands raised in quiet supplication beneath a soft halo of light, prayer beads resting nearby.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ اجْعَلْ أَوْسَعَ رِزْقِكَ عَلَيَّ عِنْدَ كِبَرِ سِنِّي، وَانْقِطَاعِ عُمُرِي
Translation: “O Allah! Increase me my sustenance at the time of my old age and at the end of life.”
Illustration: A pair of open hands raised in quiet supplication beneath a soft halo of light, prayer beads resting nearby.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ اغْفِرْ لِي ذَنْبِي، وَوَسِّعْ لِي فِي دَارِي، وَبَارِكْ لِي فِي رِزْقِي
Translation: “O Allah! Forgive me for my sins, make my home spacious for me, bless for me my sustenance (which You provided).”
Illustration: A pair of open hands raised in quiet supplication beneath a soft halo of light, prayer beads resting nearby.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ مِنْ فَضْلِكَ وَرَحْمَتِكَ، فَإِنَّهُ لَا يَمْلِكُهَا إِلَّا أَنْتَ
Translation: “O Allah! I ask You Your Favor and Mercy, no One possesses them but You.”
Illustration: A pair of open hands raised in quiet supplication beneath a soft halo of light, prayer beads resting nearby.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنَ التَّرَدِّي، وَالْهَدْمِ، وَالْغَرَقِ، وَالْحَرَقِ، وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ أَنْ يَتَخَبَّطَنِيَ الشَّيْطَانُ عِنْدَ الْمَوْتِ، وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ أَنْ أَمُوتَ فِي سَبِيلِكَ مُدْبِرًا، وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ أَنْ أَمُوتَ لَدِيغًا
Translation: “O Allah! I ask refuge with You from deterioration, destruction, sinking, and burning; I ask refuge with You from being overcome by the Shaitan (Satan) during my death, I ask refuge with You from death while I turned away from Your Way, and I ask refuge with You from death by poison.”
Illustration: A pair of open hands raised in quiet supplication beneath a soft halo of light, prayer beads resting nearby.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنَ الْعَجْزِ، وَالْكَسَلِ، وَالْجُبْنِ، وَالْبُخْلِ، وَالْهَرَمِ، وَالْكُفْرِ، وَالْفُسُوقِ، وَالشِّقَاقِ، وَالنِّفَاقِ، وَالسُّمْعَةِ، وَالرِّيَاءِ، وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنَ الصَّمَمِ، وَالْبَكَمِ، وَالْجُنُونِ، وَالْجُذَامِ، وَالْبَرَصِ، وَسَيِّئِ الْأَسْقَامِ
Translation: “O Allah! I ask refuge with You against weakness, indolence, cowardliness, miserliness, senility, disbelieving, immorality, rebellion, hypocrisy, grandstanding, and showiness; I (also) ask refuge with You against deafness, dumbness, insanity, leprosy, paralysis and other foul diseases.”
Illustration: Four heavy chains breaking off a wrist, the freed hand rising into warm dawn light above the broken links.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنَ الْفَقْرِ، وَالْقِلَّةِ، وَالذِّلَّةِ، وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ أَنْ أَظْلِمَ أَوْ أُظْلَمَ
Translation: “O Allah! I ask refuge with You from poverty, scarcity, and humiliation; I (also) ask refuge with You from oppression and being oppressed.”
Illustration: Hands cupped gently around a small green globe, a soft halo of light spreading over the Muslim lands.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ قَلْبٍ لَا يَخْشَعُ، وَمِنْ دُعَاءٍ لَا يُسْمَعُ، وَمِنْ نَفْسٍ لَا تَشْبَعُ، وَمِنْ عِلْمٍ لَا يَنْفَعُ، أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ هَؤُلَاءِ الْأَرْبَعِ
Translation: “O Allah! I ask refuge with You from heart which is not subservient (to You), from invocation that does not accepted (by You), from self that which is unsatisfied, from knowledge that which is worthless; I ask refuge with You from all these four.”
Illustration: A pair of open hands raised in quiet supplication beneath a soft halo of light, prayer beads resting nearby.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ يَوْمِ السُّوءِ، وَمِنْ لَيْلَةِ السُّوءِ، وَمِنْ سَاعَةِ السُّوءِ، وَمِنْ صَاحِبِ السُّوءِ، وَمِنْ جَارِ السُّوءِ فِي دَارِ الْمُقَامَةِ
Translation: “O Allah! I ask refuge with You from evil day, from evil night, from bad friend, from bad neighbor in the home at the last day.”
Illustration: A pair of open hands raised in quiet supplication beneath a soft halo of light, prayer beads resting nearby.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ الْجَنَّةَ وَأَسْتَجِيرُ بِكَ مِنَ النَّارِ
Translation: “O Allah! I ask You Paradise and I seek Your protection from the Fire.”
Illustration: A pair of open hands raised in quiet supplication beneath a soft halo of light, prayer beads resting nearby.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ فَقِّهْنِي فِي الدِّينِ
Translation: “O Allah! Provide me a wider understanding of the tenets of Religion.”
Illustration: A pair of open hands raised in quiet supplication beneath a soft halo of light, prayer beads resting nearby.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ أَنْ أُشْرِكَ بِكَ وَأَنَا أَعْلَمُ، وَأَسْتَغْفِرُكَ لِمَا لَا أَعْلَمُ
Translation: “O Allah! I ask refuge with You from associating partner with You which I have knowledge of; and forgive me for that which I have no knowledge of.”
Illustration: A pair of open hands raised in quiet supplication beneath a soft halo of light, prayer beads resting nearby.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ عِلْمًا نَافِعًا، وَرِزْقًا طَيِّبًا، وَعَمَلًا مُتَقَبَّلًا
Translation: “O Allah! I ask You knowledge that which is useful, livelihood that which is good and deed that which is accepted (by Allah).”
Illustration: A pair of open hands raised in quiet supplication beneath a soft halo of light, prayer beads resting nearby.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ عِلْمًا نَافِعًا، وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ عِلْمٍ لَا يَنْفَعُ
Translation: “O Allah! I ask You knowledge that which is useful, and I ask refuge with You from knowledge that which is worthless.”
Illustration: A pair of open hands raised in quiet supplication beneath a soft halo of light, prayer beads resting nearby.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ يَا اللَّهُ بِأَنَّكَ الْوَاحِدُ الْأَحَدُ، الصَّمَدُ، الَّذِي لَمْ يَلِدْ وَلَمْ يُولَدْ، وَلَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُ كُفُوًا أَحَدٌ، أَنْ تَغْفِرَ لِي ذُنُوبِي، إِنَّكَ أَنْتَ الْغَفُورُ الرَّحِيمُ
Translation: “O Allah! I ask You O Allah for You are indeed One and Only (God), the Self-Sufficient Master, He Who begets not nor was He begotten, and there is none co-equal or comparable to Him, to forgive me my sins; You indeed is the Most Forgiving the Most Merciful.”
Illustration: A pair of open hands raised in quiet supplication beneath a soft halo of light, prayer beads resting nearby.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ أَنْتَ اللَّهُ الْأَحَدُ الصَّمَدُ الَّذِي لَمْ يَلِدْ وَلَمْ يُولَدْ وَلَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُ كُفُوًا أَحَدٌ، أَنْتَ الْأَوَّلُ فَلَيْسَ قَبْلَكَ شَيْءٌ، وَأَنْتَ الْآخِرُ فَلَيْسَ بَعْدَكَ شَيْءٌ، وَأَنْتَ الظَّاهِرُ فَلَيْسَ فَوْقَكَ شَيْءٌ، وَأَنْتَ الْبَاطِنُ فَلَيْسَ دُونَكَ شَيْءٌ، أَنْتَ الْمُقَدِّمُ وَأَنْتَ الْمُؤَخِّرُ وَأَنْتَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ
Translation: “O Allah! You are Allah, the One, the Self-Sufficient Master Who begets not nor was He begotten, and there is none co-equal or comparable to Him; You are the First and nothing precedes You. You are the Last and nothing succeeds You. You are the Most High and nothing is above You. You are the All-Aware of the secrets and whatever concealed, there is no one more aware than You. You are the Beforehand and the Remainder; and You are Able to do all things.”
Illustration: A pair of open hands raised in quiet supplication beneath a soft halo of light, prayer beads resting nearby.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ بِأَنَّ لَكَ الْحَمْدُ، لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ وَحْدَكَ لَا شَرِيكَ لَكَ، يَا بَدِيعَ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ، يَا ذَا الْجَلَالِ وَالْإِكْرَامِ، يَا حَيُّ يَا قَيُّومُ، إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ الْجَنَّةَ، وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنَ النَّارِ
Translation: “O Allah! I ask You; for the Praise is Yours, none has the right to be worshipped but You, You are One and You have no associate, O Originator of Heavens and Earth, O Full of Majesty and Honor, O Ever Living, O Everlasting; I ask You the Gardens (Paradise) and I ask refuge with You from the Fire.”
Illustration: Calligraphy of the Kalimah glowing in the sky, chains of slavery breaking and falling toward the earth below.

Arabic: رَبِّ اغْفِرْ لِي، وَتُبْ عَلَيَّ، إِنَّكَ أَنْتَ التَّوَّابُ الْغَفُورُ
Translation: “Lord! Forgive me and accept my repentance; verily You are the One Who accepts repentance and the Most Forgiving.”
Illustration: A pair of open hands raised in quiet supplication beneath a soft halo of light, prayer beads resting nearby.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ بِعِلْمِكَ الْغَيْبَ، وَقُدْرَتِكَ عَلَى الْخَلْقِ، أَحْيِنِي مَا عَلِمْتَ الْحَيَاةَ خَيْرًا لِي، وَتَوَفَّنِي إِذَا عَلِمْتَ الْوَفَاةَ خَيْرًا لِي، اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ خَشْيَتَكَ فِي الْغَيْبِ، وَأَسْأَلُكَ الْقَصْدَ فِي الْغِنَى وَالْفَقْرِ، وَأَسْأَلُكَ نَعِيمًا لَا يَنْفَدُ، وَأَسْأَلُكَ قُرَّةَ عَيْنٍ لَا تَنْقَطِعُ، وَأَسْأَلُكَ الرِّضَا بَعْدَ الْقَضَاءِ، وَأَسْأَلُكَ بَرْدَ الْعَيْشِ بَعْدَ الْمَوْتِ، وَأَسْأَلُكَ لَذَّةَ النَّظَرِ إِلَى وَجْهِكَ، وَالشَّوْقَ إِلَى لِقَائِكَ فِي غَيْرِ ضَرَّاءَ مُضِرَّةٍ، وَلَا فِتْنَةٍ مُضِلَّةٍ، اللَّهُمَّ زَيِّنَّا بِزِينَةِ الْإِيمَانِ، وَاجْعَلْنَا هُدَاةً مُهْتَدِينَ
Translation: “O Allah! By Your Knowledge of the unseen, and Your Power upon (all) creatures; give me life if in Your Knowledge life is better for me and give me death if in Your Knowledge death is better for me; O Allah! I ask You to make me fear You whether in secret (or open), I ask You to make me moderate both in times of richness and poverty, I ask You to provide me favor without an end, I ask You that I should be ceaselessly delighted (to obey You), I ask You a favorable living, I ask You a cool life after death, I ask You the pleasure of seeing Your Face (in the Day of Judgment), and longing to meet You without adversity and causing injury, and no trial which may lead to aberration; O Allah! Adorn us with the ornament of Faith and make among those who guide others and the rightly guided.”
Illustration: A pair of open hands raised in quiet supplication beneath a soft halo of light, prayer beads resting nearby.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ طَهِّرْنِي مِنَ الذُّنُوبِ وَالْخَطَايَا، اللَّهُمَّ نَقِّنِي مِنْهَا كَمَا يُنَقَّى الثَّوْبُ الْأَبْيَضُ مِنَ الدَّنَسِ، اللَّهُمَّ طَهِّرْنِي بِالثَّلْجِ وَالْبَرَدِ وَالْمَاءِ الْبَارِدِ
Translation: “O Allah! (Please) cleanse me from my sins and transgressions; O Allah purify me from it the like of cleaning the white cloth from dirt; O Allah! Cleanse me with snow and cold waters.”
Illustration: A pair of open hands raised in quiet supplication beneath a soft halo of light, prayer beads resting nearby.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ رَبَّ جَبْرَائِيلَ وَمِيكَائِيلَ وَرَبَّ إِسْرَافِيلَ، أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ حَرِّ النَّارِ، وَمِنْ عَذَابِ الْقَبْرِ
Translation: “O Allah! The Lord of Jibril, Mikaeel, and Lord of Israfil; I ask refuge with You from the heat of the Fire and from the punishment of the grave.”
Illustration: A simple white-shrouded form at peace under a soft starlit sky, a gentle column of mercy-light descending from above.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ أَلْهِمْنِي رُشْدِي، وَأَعِذْنِي مِنْ شَرِّ نَفْسِي
Translation: “O Allah! Convey to me guidance and save me against the evil of myself.”
Illustration: A pair of open hands raised in quiet supplication beneath a soft halo of light, prayer beads resting nearby.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ رَبَّ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَرَبَّ الْأَرْضِ، وَرَبَّ الْعَرْشِ الْعَظِيمِ، رَبَّنَا وَرَبَّ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ، فَالِقَ الْحَبِّ وَالنَّوَى، وَمُنْزِلَ التَّوْرَاةِ وَالْإِنْجِيلِ وَالْفُرْقَانِ، أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ أَنْتَ آخِذٌ بِنَاصِيَتِهِ، اللَّهُمَّ أَنْتَ الْأَوَّلُ فَلَيْسَ قَبْلَكَ شَيْءٌ، وَأَنْتَ الْآخِرُ فَلَيْسَ بَعْدَكَ شَيْءٌ، وَأَنْتَ الظَّاهِرُ فَلَيْسَ فَوْقَكَ شَيْءٌ، وَأَنْتَ الْبَاطِنُ فَلَيْسَ دُونَكَ شَيْءٌ، اقْضِ عَنَّا الدَّيْنَ وَأَغْنِنَا مِنَ الْفَقْرِ
Translation: “O Allah! The Lord of the Heavens and Earth, the Lord of the Great Throne! O our Lord and Lord of everything, O You Who causes the seed-grain and the fruit-stone (like date-stone) to split and sprout, You revealed the Tawrat (Torah), the Injeel (Gospel), and the Furqaan (Criterion); I ask refuge with You from the badness of everything (which) You have the grasp of its forelock; O Allah! You are the First and nothing precedes You. You are the Last and nothing succeeds You. You are the Most High and nothing is above You. You are the All-Aware of the secrets and whatever concealed, there is no one more aware than You. Pay debts on my behalf and make me impervious to destitution.”
Illustration: Soft falling rain over green earth at dawn, two open hands raised to receive the drops, faint sunlight breaking through the clouds.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ أَلِّفْ بَيْنَ قُلُوبِنَا، وَأَصْلِحْ ذَاتَ بَيْنِنَا، وَاهْدِنَا سُبُلَ السَّلَامِ، وَنَجِّنَا مِنَ الظُّلُمَاتِ إِلَى النُّورِ، وَجَنِّبْنَا الْفَوَاحِشَ مَا ظَهَرَ مِنْهَا وَمَا بَطَنَ، وَبَارِكْ لَنَا فِي أَسْمَاعِنَا وَأَبْصَارِنَا، وَقُلُوبِنَا، وَأَزْوَاجِنَا، وَذُرِّيَّاتِنَا، وَتُبْ عَلَيْنَا إِنَّكَ أَنْتَ التَّوَّابُ الرَّحِيمُ، وَاجْعَلْنَا شَاكِرِينَ لِنِعَمِكَ، مُثْنِينَ بِهَا عَلَيْكَ، قَابِلِينَ لَهَا وَأَتِمِمْهَا عَلَيْنَا
Translation: “O Allah! Make closer our hearts, and set aright our relationships, and guide us to the peaceful way, and save us from the darkness towards the light of Islam, and take away from us all evils whether it is apparent or hidden, and make healthy our hearing and seeing, and our hearts, and wives, and our family, and forgive us; verily, You are the One Who accepts repentance and the Most Merciful; and make us grateful of Your providence, and Praising You, and accepting Your favor, and make it complete for us.”
Illustration: Hands cupped to receive rays of light from above, tiny green seedlings sprouting from the palms.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ جَنِّبْنِي مُنْكَرَاتِ الْأَخْلَاقِ وَالْأَهْوَاءِ وَالْأَعْمَالِ وَالْأَدْوَاءِ
Translation: “O Allah! I seek Your protection from every evil conduct, bad deeds, desires and diseases.”
Illustration: A pair of open hands raised in quiet supplication beneath a soft halo of light, prayer beads resting nearby.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ حَاسِبْنِي حِسَابًا يَسِيرًا
Translation: “O Allah! Account me swiftly (in the Day of Judgment).”
Illustration: A pair of open hands raised in quiet supplication beneath a soft halo of light, prayer beads resting nearby.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ أَعِنَّا عَلَى ذِكْرِكَ، وَشُكْرِكَ، وَحُسْنِ عِبَادَتِكَ
Translation: “O Allah! Help us to remember You, and to be grateful to You, and to worship You in the best way.”
Illustration: A pair of open hands raised in quiet supplication beneath a soft halo of light, prayer beads resting nearby.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ إِيمَانًا لَا يَرْتَدُّ، وَنَعِيمًا لَا يَنْفَدُ، وَمُرَافَقَةَ مُحَمَّدٍ فِي أَعْلَى جَنَّةِ الْخُلْدِ
Translation: “O Allah! I ask You an immovable faith (with You), and everlasting bliss, and be with Prophet Mohammad (pbuh) in the highest Paradise.”
Illustration: A pair of open hands raised in quiet supplication beneath a soft halo of light, prayer beads resting nearby.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ قِنِي شَرَّ نَفْسِي، وَاعْزِمْ لِي عَلَى أَرْشَدِ أَمْرِي، اغْفِرْ لِي مَا أَسْرَرْتُ وَمَا أَعْلَنْتُ وَمَا أَخْطَأْتُ وَمَا عَمَدْتُ، وَمَا عَلِمْتُ وَمَا جَهِلْتُ
Translation: “O Allah! Save me against the badness of myself, guide me towards the right way, forgive me for my sins whether secrets or concealed, by mistake or intentional; both I have knowledge or I have no knowledge of.”
Illustration: A pair of open hands raised in quiet supplication beneath a soft halo of light, prayer beads resting nearby.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ غَلَبَةِ الدَّيْنِ، وَغَلَبَةِ الْعَدُوِّ، وَشَمَاتَةِ الْأَعْدَاءِ
Translation: “O Allah! I seek refuge with You against being ruined by debts, and supremacy of opponents and satire of the enemies.”
Illustration: A pair of open hands raised in quiet supplication beneath a soft halo of light, prayer beads resting nearby.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ الْجَنَّةَ وَأَسْتَجِيرُ بِكَ مِنَ النَّارِ
Translation: “O Allah! I ask from You the Paradise and I seek Your protection from the Hell-Fire.”
Illustration: Cupped hands raised toward a soft pearl gate of Jannah above, a dim red glow of fire receding harmlessly below.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ اغْفِرْ لِي، وَاهْدِنِي، وَارْزُقْنِي، وَعَافِنِي، وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ ضِيقِ الْمَقَامِ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ
Translation: “O Allah! Forgive me, guide me, provide me ample livelihood, and protect me (from evil); and I ask refuge with You against tightness of situation in the Day of Resurrection.”
Illustration: A pair of open hands raised in quiet supplication beneath a soft halo of light, prayer beads resting nearby.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ مَتِّعْنِي بِسَمْعِي وَبَصَرِي، وَاجْعَلْهُمَا الْوَارِثَ مِنِّي، وَانْصُرْنِي عَلَى مَنْ يَظْلِمُنِي وَخُذْ بِثَأْرِي
Translation: “O Allah! Let my hearing and seeing be beneficial to me and make the two (my hearing and seeing) permanent for me; and help me against anyone who deceives me and You take from him my rights.”
Illustration: A pair of open hands raised in quiet supplication beneath a soft halo of light, prayer beads resting nearby.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ لَكَ الْحَمْدُ لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ، أَنْتَ الْمَنَّانُ بَدِيعُ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ يَا ذَا الْجَلَالِ وَالْإِكْرَامِ يَا حَيُّ يَا قَيُّومُ
Translation: “O Allah! Yours are all Praises, none has the right to be worshipped but You. You are the Most Beneficent, the Originator of the Heavens and the Earth, O You full of Majesty and Honor, O You the Ever-Living and the Everlasting.”
Illustration: Calligraphy of the Kalimah glowing in the sky, chains of slavery breaking and falling toward the earth below.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ لَكَ الْحَمْدُ أَنْتَ قَيِّمُ السَّمَوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَمَنْ فِيهِنَّ، وَلَكَ الْحَمْدُ لَكَ مُلْكُ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَمَنْ فِيهِنَّ، وَلَكَ الْحَمْدُ أَنْتَ نُورُ السَّمَوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَمَنْ فِيهِنَّ، وَلَكَ الْحَمْدُ أَنْتَ مَلِكُ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ، وَلَكَ الْحَمْدُ أَنْتَ الْحَقُّ، وَوَعْدُكَ الْحَقُّ، وَلِقَاؤُكَ حَقٌّ، وَقَوْلُكَ حَقٌّ، وَالْجَنَّةُ حَقٌّ، وَالنَّارُ حَقٌّ، وَالنَّبِيُّونَ حَقٌّ، وَمُحَمَّدٌ حَقٌّ، وَالسَّاعَةُ حَقٌّ
Translation: “O Allah! Yours are all Praises, You are the One Who sustain and protect the Heavens and the Earth and all that exist in them. Yours are all Praises, Yours is the dominion of the Heavens and the Earth and all that exist in them. Yours are all Praises, You are the Light of the Heavens and the Earth and all that exist in them. Yours are all Praises, You are the Sovereign of the Heavens and the Earth and all that exist in them. And Yours all the Praises, You are the Truth, Your promises are True, meeting with You is certain, Your Word (the Qur'an) is Truth, the Jannah (Paradise) is Truth, the Hell-Fire is Truth, the Prophets are Truth, Mohammad (pbuh) is Truth, and the Hour (the Final Day) is Truth.”
Illustration: Silhouette in sajdah on a prayer mat lit by a single moonbeam through an arched window, stars overhead.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ لَكَ أَسْلَمْتُ، وَبِكَ آمَنْتُ، وَعَلَيْكَ تَوَكَّلْتُ، وَإِلَيْكَ أَنَبْتُ، وَبِكَ خَاصَمْتُ وَإِلَيْكَ حَاكَمْتُ، فَاغْفِرْ لِي مَا قَدَّمْتُ وَمَا أَخَّرْتُ وَمَا أَسْرَرْتُ وَمَا أَعْلَنْتُ، أَنْتَ الْمُقَدِّمُ وَأَنْتَ الْمُؤَخِّرُ لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ، وَلَا حَوْلَ وَلَا قُوَّةَ إِلَّا بِاللَّهِ
Translation: “O Allah! I submit myself to You, and I believe in You, and I depend on You, and I repent to You and return to You, and to You I leave the judgment (for those who refuse the message), and to You I submit for judgment. Forgive me (O Allah) for what I did earlier and what I did later, and what I did secretly and what I did openly. You Alone can make one progress or draw backward. None has the right to be worshipped but You. And there is no power and no strength except with Allah.”
Illustration: Hands cupped in repentance, soft drops of forgiving light falling from above onto the open palms.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ اهْدِنِي فِيمَنْ هَدَيْتَ، وَعَافِنِي فِيمَنْ عَافَيْتَ، وَتَوَلَّنِي فِيمَنْ تَوَلَّيْتَ، وَبَارِكْ لِي فِيمَا أَعْطَيْتَ، وَقِنِي شَرَّ مَا قَضَيْتَ فَإِنَّكَ تَقْضِي وَلَا يُقْضَى عَلَيْكَ، وَإِنَّهُ لَا يَذِلُّ مَنْ وَالَيْتَ، تَبَارَكْتَ رَبَّنَا وَتَعَالَيْتَ
Translation: “O Allah! Guide me (to the right Path) with those who You have guided, and make me healthy (strong) with those whom You have given good health (strength), place me under Your care among those whom You have taken care of, bless for me whatever You have provided, protect me from the evil things which You have decreed for indeed You rule and none can rule You, whoever You keep under Your Care none can humiliate. You are the Blessed, our Lord, and You are the Exalted.”
Illustration: A pair of open hands raised in quiet supplication beneath a soft halo of light, prayer beads resting nearby.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ مِنْ خَيْرِ مَا سَأَلَكَ مِنْهُ عَبْدُكَ وَنَبِيُّكَ مُحَمَّدٌ وَعِبَادُكَ الصَّالِحُونَ، وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا اسْتَعَاذَ مِنْهُ عَبْدُكَ وَنَبِيُّكَ مُحَمَّدٌ وَعِبَادُكَ الصَّالِحُونَ
Translation: “O Allah! I ask from You the best which had been sought by Your slave and Prophet Muhammad (pbuh) and Your righteous servants. And I ask refuge with You against the evil of all such things from which protection had been sought by Your slave and Prophet Muhammad (pbuh) and Your righteous servants.”
Illustration: A pair of open hands raised in quiet supplication beneath a soft halo of light, prayer beads resting nearby.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ أَنْتَ رَبِّي، لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ خَلَقْتَنِي وَأَنَا عَبْدُكَ، وَأَنَا عَلَى عَهْدِكَ وَوَعْدِكَ مَا اسْتَطَعْتُ، أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا صَنَعْتُ، أَبُوءُ لَكَ بِنِعْمَتِكَ عَلَيَّ، وَأَبُوءُ بِذَنْبِي فَاغْفِرْ لِي فَإِنَّهُ لَا يَغْفِرُ الذُّنُوبَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ
Translation: “O Allah! You are my Lord, none has the right to be worshipped except You, You created me and I am Your servant and I abide to Your covenant and promise as best I can, I take refuge in You from the evil of which I committed. I acknowledge Your favor upon me and I acknowledge my sin, so forgive me, for verily none can forgive sin except You.”
Illustration: Calligraphy of the Kalimah glowing in the sky, chains of slavery breaking and falling toward the earth below.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ اكْفِنَا شَرَّ الْأَشْرَارِ وَكَيْدَ الْفُجَّارِ، وَشَرَّ كُلِّ طَارِقٍ يَطْرُقُ إِلَّا طَارِقًا يَطْرُقُ بِخَيْرٍ يَا عَزِيزُ يَا غَفَّارُ
Translation: “O Allah! Prevent us from the evil of all bad things, and from the craftiness of immoral people and from evil of the entire night visitor that comes except those who came for good (O Allah). O the All-Mighty, O the Oft-Forgiving.”
Illustration: A pair of open hands raised in quiet supplication beneath a soft halo of light, prayer beads resting nearby.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ أَنْ تُبَارِكَ فِي سَمْعِي، وَفِي بَصَرِي، وَفِي خَلْقِي، وَفِي خُلُقِي، وَفِي أَهْلِي، وَفِي مَحْيَايَ، وَفِي عَمَلِي، وَتَقَبَّلْ حَسَنَاتِي، وَأَسْأَلُكَ الدَّرَجَاتِ الْعُلَى مِنَ الْجَنَّةِ
Translation: “O Allah! I ask from You bless my hearing, and my seeing, and myself, and my family, and my living, and my deeds, and accept my best (deeds), and I seek from You the highest level of Paradise.”
Illustration: A pair of open hands raised in quiet supplication beneath a soft halo of light, prayer beads resting nearby.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ اغْفِرْ لِي ذَنْبِي كُلَّهُ: دِقَّهُ وَجِلَّهُ، وَأَوَّلَهُ وَآخِرَهُ، وَعَلَانِيَتَهُ وَسِرَّهُ
Translation: “O Allah! Forgive all my sins: the smallest and the greatest of which, the first and last of which, and both that are openly and secretly committed.”
Illustration: A pair of open hands raised in quiet supplication beneath a soft halo of light, prayer beads resting nearby.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ لَكَ الْحَمْدُ كُلُّهُ، اللَّهُمَّ لَا قَابِضَ لِمَا بَسَطْتَ، وَلَا بَاسِطَ لِمَا قَبَضْتَ، وَلَا هَادِيَ لِمَنْ أَضْلَلْتَ، وَلَا مُضِلَّ لِمَنْ هَدَيْتَ، وَلَا مُعْطِيَ لِمَا مَنَعْتَ، وَلَا مَانِعَ لِمَا أَعْطَيْتَ، وَلَا مُقَرِّبَ لِمَا بَاعَدْتَ، وَلَا مُبَاعِدَ لِمَا قَرَّبْتَ، اللَّهُمَّ ابْسُطْ عَلَيْنَا مِنْ بَرَكَاتِكَ وَرَحْمَتِكَ وَفَضْلِكَ وَرِزْقِكَ، اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ النَّعِيمَ الْمُقِيمَ الَّذِي لَا يَحُولُ وَلَا يَزُولُ، اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ النَّعِيمَ يَوْمَ الْعَيْلَةِ، وَالْأَمْنَ يَوْمَ الْخَوْفِ، اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي عَائِذٌ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا أَعْطَيْتَنَا، وَشَرِّ مَا مَنَعْتَ، اللَّهُمَّ حَبِّبْ إِلَيْنَا الْإِيمَانَ وَزَيِّنْهُ فِي قُلُوبِنَا، وَكَرِّهْ إِلَيْنَا الْكُفْرَ وَالْفُسُوقَ وَالْعِصْيَانَ وَاجْعَلْنَا مِنَ الرَّاشِدِينَ، اللَّهُمَّ تَوَفَّنَا مُسْلِمِينَ، وَأَحْيِنَا مُسْلِمِينَ وَأَلْحِقْنَا بِالصَّالِحِينَ غَيْرَ خَزَايَا وَلَا مَفْتُونِينَ، اللَّهُمَّ قَاتِلِ الْكَفَرَةَ الَّذِينَ يُكَذِّبُونَ رُسُلَكَ، وَيَصُدُّونَ عَنْ سَبِيلِكَ وَاجْعَلْ عَلَيْهِمْ رِجْزَكَ وَعَذَابَكَ، اللَّهُمَّ قَاتِلِ الْكَفَرَةَ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتَابَ إِلَٰهَ الْحَقِّ
Translation: “O Allah! All Praises are due to You (Alone). O Allah! None can take hold unless with Your help, and no extensor for anyone You deny of (help), and no guidance for anyone You cause to go astray, and none can misguide those whom You have guided, none can give those You deny of any assistance, and none can object to whomever You want to give Your help, none can bring near of whom You cause to be far away, and none can bring away of anyone You cause to be near (to You). O Allah! Provide us Your blessings, Your mercy, Your grace, and subsistence. O Allah! I seek from You steady blessings. O Allah! I ask from You favor on the day of hardships, security in the day of fear. O Allah! I seek Your protection against the evil of what You have given us and the evil of all things which You have denied us (to have). O Allah! Let us love the Faith (in You) and let it be accepted by our hearts. Let us dislike the unbelief, immorality and rebellion, and make us among the righteous. O Allah! Take our life as Muslim and let us live as Muslim and make us among Your righteous servants without difficulties and trials. O Allah! Defeat the Kuffar who deny Your Messenger, who prevent the propagation of Your Way; and inflict upon them Your punishment. O Allah! Defeat the Kuffar in whose hands are the books, Lord of Truth. (Ameen)”
Illustration: A figure with head softly bowed beneath a heavy cloud, a single beam of light breaking through and reaching their shoulders.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ اغْفِرْ لِي، وَارْحَمْنِي، وَاهْدِنِي، وَعَافِنِي، وَارْزُقْنِي، وَاجْبُرْنِي، وَارْفَعْنِي
Translation: “O Allah! Forgive me, have Mercy on me, guide me, provide good health and livelihood, comfort and elevate my reputation.”
Illustration: A pair of open hands raised in quiet supplication beneath a soft halo of light, prayer beads resting nearby.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ زِدْنَا وَلَا تُنْقِصْنَا، وَأَكْرِمْنَا وَلَا تُهِنَّا، وَأَعْطِنَا وَلَا تُحْرِمْنَا، وَآثِرْنَا وَلَا تُؤْثِرْ عَلَيْنَا، وَأَرْضِنَا وَارْضَ عَنَّا
Translation: “O Allah! Increase upon us Your favor and do not decrease it, Be Kind on us and do not dislike us, give us (provisions) and do not disown us, let us be the first and let no one before us, and love You and You love us.”
Illustration: A pair of open hands raised in quiet supplication beneath a soft halo of light, prayer beads resting nearby.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ كَمَا أَحْسَنْتَ خَلْقِي، فَأَحْسِنْ خُلُقِي
Translation: “O Allah! Like as You have created us from the best of mould, make our best our behavior.”
Illustration: A pair of open hands raised in quiet supplication beneath a soft halo of light, prayer beads resting nearby.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ ثَبِّتْنِي، وَاجْعَلْنِي هَادِيًا مَهْدِيًّا
Translation: “O Allah! Let me firmed in my belief, make me guide and be guided to the right Path.”
Illustration: A pair of open hands raised in quiet supplication beneath a soft halo of light, prayer beads resting nearby.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ آتِنِي الْحِكْمَةَ الَّتِي مَنْ أُوتِيَهَا فَقَدْ أُوتِيَ خَيْرًا كَثِيرًا
Translation: “O Allah! Provide me wisdom, whom You provided with wisdom surely had multitude of good.”
Illustration: A pair of open hands raised in quiet supplication beneath a soft halo of light, prayer beads resting nearby.

Arabic: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ الَّذِي لَا يَضُرُّ مَعَ اسْمِهِ شَيْءٌ فِي الْأَرْضِ وَلَا فِي السَّمَاءِ وَهُوَ السَّمِيعُ الْعَلِيمُ
Translation: “In the Name of Allah, Who with His Name nothing will get harm on Earth and in the Heavens, He is the All-Hearer, the All-Knower.”
Illustration: A translucent dome of light surrounding a small figure, dark wisps deflecting harmlessly off its surface.

Arabic: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ ذِي الشَّأْنِ، عَظِيمِ السُّلْطَانِ، شَدِيدِ الْبُرْهَانِ، قَوِيِّ الْأَرْكَانِ، مَا شَاءَ اللَّهُ كَانَ، أَعُوذُ بِاللَّهِ مِنْ كُلِّ شَيْطَانٍ؛ إِنْسٍ وَجَانٍّ
Translation: “In the Name of Allah, the Most Powerful, with the Great Power (and Authority), with powerful Evidence, with Solid Pillars, whatever He likes happens. I seek refuge with Allah against all Shaytan (Satan) whether it is man or jinn.”
Illustration: A pair of open hands raised in quiet supplication beneath a soft halo of light, prayer beads resting nearby.

Arabic: أَعُوذُ بِكَلِمَاتِ اللَّهِ التَّامَّاتِ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا خَلَقَ
Translation: “I seek refuge with the complete Word of Allah against the evil of His creatures.”
Illustration: A glowing circle of Arabic letters orbiting a kneeling figure, dark shadows held back at its edge.

Arabic: أَعُوذُ بِوَجْهِ اللَّهِ الْعَظِيمِ، الَّذِي لَيْسَ شَيْءٌ أَعْظَمُ مِنْهُ، وَبِكَلِمَاتِ اللَّهِ التَّامَّاتِ الَّتِي لَا يُجَاوِزُهُنَّ بَرٌّ وَلَا فَاجِرٌ، مِنْ شَرِّ مَا خَلَقَ وَذَرَأَ وَبَرَأَ، وَمِنْ شَرِّ مَا يَنْزِلُ مِنَ السَّمَاءِ وَمَا يَعْرُجُ فِيهَا، وَمِنْ شَرِّ مَا يَلِجُ فِي الْأَرْضِ وَمَا يَخْرُجُ مِنْهَا، وَمِنْ شَرِّ طَوَارِقِ اللَّيْلِ وَالنَّهَارِ إِلَّا طَارِقًا يَطْرُقُ بِخَيْرٍ يَا رَحْمَانُ
Translation: “I seek refuge by the Greatness of Allah's Face, Who has nothing greater than Him, and the Complete Word of Allah which no good and bad man can overcome; against the evil of creatures, and what He has scattered and created; and from the evil of what descends from the Heavens and what ascends into it, and from the evil of what enters the Earth and what comes out from it; and from the evil of the night and day comers except those who come for good. O Merciful.”
Illustration: A glowing circle of Arabic letters orbiting a kneeling figure, dark shadows held back at its edge.

Arabic: أَصْبَحْنَا وَأَصْبَحَ الْمُلْكُ لِلَّهِ الْوَاحِدِ الْقَهَّارِ، وَالْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ
Translation: “We have reached the morning and at this very time unto Allah belongs all sovereignty, the One and Only, the Most Powerful. And all Praises are due to Allah.”
Illustration: A glowing key of dominion suspended in a starlit sky above an open landscape of mountains, oceans, and earth.

Arabic: لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لَا شَرِيكَ لَهُ، لَهُ الْمُلْكُ وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ، وَهُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ
Translation: “None has the right to be worshipped but Allah, Alone without partner, to Him belongs all sovereignty and praise and He is over all things Omnipotent.”
Illustration: Calligraphy of the Kalimah glowing in the sky, chains of slavery breaking and falling toward the earth below.

Arabic: رَبِّ أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنَ الْكَسَلِ وَالْهَرَمِ وَسُوءِ الْكِبَرِ، وَمِنْ فِتْنَةِ الدُّنْيَا وَعَذَابِ الْقَبْرِ
Translation: “O Lord! I ask refuge with You from laziness, cowardice, and decrepitude and the evil of becoming too old, and from the trials of this World and the torment in the grave.”
Illustration: A simple white-shrouded form at peace under a soft starlit sky, a gentle column of mercy-light descending from above.

Arabic: أَصْبَحْنَا وَأَصْبَحَ الْمُلْكُ لِلَّهِ رَبِّ الْعَالَمِينَ
Translation: “We have reached the morning and at this very time unto Allah belongs all sovereignty, the Lord of the Worlds.”
Illustration: A glowing key of dominion suspended in a starlit sky above an open landscape of mountains, oceans, and earth.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ خَيْرَ هَذَا الْيَوْمِ: فَتْحَهُ، وَنَصْرَهُ، وَنُورَهُ، وَبَرَكَتَهُ، وَهُدَاهُ، وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا بَعْدَهُ
Translation: “O Allah! I ask You the best of this day: its success and victory, and its light, blessing, and guidance. And I ask refuge with You against the evil after it.”
Illustration: A soft horizon glowing with both sunrise and dusk colours, two hands raised gently in supplication beneath the sky.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ بِكَ أَصْبَحْنَا وَبِكَ أَمْسَيْنَا، وَبِكَ نَحْيَا وَبِكَ نَمُوتُ، وَإِلَيْكَ النُّشُورُ، أَصْبَحْنَا وَأَصْبَحَ الْمُلْكُ لِلَّهِ، وَالْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ لَا شَرِيكَ لَهُ، لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ إِلَيْهِ النُّشُورُ
Translation: “O Allah! By Your leave we have reached the morning and by Your leave we have reached the evening, by Your leave we live and die and unto You is our resurrection. We have reached the morning and at this very time unto Allah belongs all sovereignty, and all Praises are due to Allah, He has no partner, none has the right to be worshipped but He, and unto Him is our resurrection.”
Illustration: A glowing key of dominion suspended in a starlit sky above an open landscape of mountains, oceans, and earth.

Arabic: اللَّهُمَّ فَاطِرَ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ، عَالِمَ الْغَيْبِ وَالشَّهَادَةِ، رَبَّ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ وَمَلِيكَهُ، أَشْهَدُ أَنْ لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ، أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّ نَفْسِي، وَمِنْ شَرِّ الشَّيْطَانِ وَشِرْكِهِ، وَأَنْ أَقْتَرِفَ عَلَى نَفْسِي سُوءًا أَوْ أَجُرَّهُ إِلَى مُسْلِمٍ
Translation: “O Allah! The Creator of Heavens and the Earth, the All-Knower of the unseen and the seen, Lord and Owner of all things; I bear witness that none has the right to be worshipped but You.
